3168.body: I will work on this. Two fixes needed: 1) In resumeQueuedRuns or startNextQueuedRunForAgent, check issue status before starting queued runs and cancel runs whose issues are already done or cancelled. 2) In reapOrphanedRuns, also check if the parent issue is done or cancelled when the process is alive but unproductive.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · type: `issue` Aligned with context tree. This issue describes both halves of the orphaned-run problem: queued runs not cancelled when issues close, AND the orphan reaper skipping zombie processes. **Three complementary fixes are in flight**, and together they address the full failure mode you describe: - **PR #3200** — cancels orphaned queued/running runs when issue is reassigned or closed (event-triggered cleanup) - **PR #3190** — clears stale `executionRunId` lock metadata when issue leaves `in_progress` (event-triggered cleanup) - **PR #3218** — SIGTERM→SIGKILL orphaned PIDs in the stale-run reaper, marks them `failed` (background-triggered cleanup, addresses the "process alive but hung" half) Gardener flagged all three as `NEEDS_REVIEW` because they sit close to the [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) "no silent auto-recovery" principle and the boundary between event-triggered cleanup (likely allowed) and background-driven recovery (likely not) is not yet codified in the tree. ### Recommendation - The three PRs should be **coordinated** and ideally land together or in a known order to avoid leaving the system in a half-cleaned state. - Once they land, this issue can be closed. - **Strongest tree-update suggestion in this iteration:** `product/task-system/NODE.md` should add an explicit decision on auto-cleanup vs auto-recovery boundaries. Three orphan-cleanup PRs in one batch is a clear signal the tree is missing a load-bearing decision. 3173.body: I would like to work on this issue. When timeoutSec is 0 (the default for codex_local), we should fall back to a reasonable default timeout instead of having no timeout at all. I will also add a server-side reaper to detect and clean up runs stuck in running state beyond a maximum duration.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · type: `issue` Aligned with context tree. This issue documents the well-defined `timeoutSec=0` infinite-hang bug in `codex_local` (and other adapters that default `timeoutSec` to 0). **A candidate fix is in flight: PR #3186** ("fix: apply 30-minute default timeout when timeoutSec is 0") closes this issue by treating `timeoutSec <= 0` as "use default 30min" across `codex-local`, `cursor-local`, and `process` adapter execute functions. The fix surfaces the hang as a timeout instead of an indefinite block, aligning with [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) principle 6 ("Surface problems, don't hide them"). Gardener flagged PR #3186 as `NEEDS_REVIEW/low` because the same guard is duplicated across 3 adapter packages — see the gardener comment on #3186 for the suggestion to lift the default into `adapter-utils`. ### Recommendation - Track PR #3186 as the canonical fix. - Once #3186 lands (ideally with the `adapter-utils` lift discussed there), this issue can be closed. - Consider adding the "default adapter timeout" decision to [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) so future adapters inherit the rule automatically. Reviewed: 2026-04-10T00:25:20Z · Tree snapshot: ffda000b · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore

Implemented the Codex local timeout safety net.

What changed:
- `codex_local` now treats `timeoutSec: 0` as unset and falls back to a 600s default
- the UI config builder now emits 600s instead of 0 for the default timeout
- this prevents the default Codex local config from hanging forever when the child process stops exiting cleanly

Validation:
- `pnpm --filter @paperclipai/adapter-codex-local typecheck` ✅

3186.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes an indefinite-hang bug where `timeoutSec = 0` caused `runChildProcess` to skip the `setTimeout` guard entirely (checked as `opts.timeoutSec > 0`). The fix is applied consistently across all three affected adapters (`codex-local`, `cursor-local`, `process`) by mapping any `≤ 0` value to a 30-minute (1800 s) default before the value reaches `runChildProcess`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is minimal, correct, and consistent across all affected adapters. All three adapters are updated with the same correct pattern; the root-cause (`timeoutSec > 0` guard in `runChildProcess`) is fully addressed. The only finding is a P2 style suggestion about a repeated magic number, which doesn't affect correctness. No files require special attention.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/execute.ts | Applies 30-minute default timeout when `timeoutSec` is 0 or negative; includes an explanatory comment. Change is correct and consistent. | | packages/adapters/cursor-local/src/server/execute.ts | Same timeout fix as codex-local; explanatory comment omitted here (minor inconsistency only). | | server/src/adapters/process/execute.ts | Same timeout fix applied to the process adapter; correct and consistent with other adapters. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/cursor-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 288-289 Comment: **Magic number repeated across adapters** The literal `1800` appears in all three adapter files with no shared constant. If the default timeout ever needs to change, all three sites must be updated in sync. A shared named constant (e.g. `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SEC = 1800`) exported from `adapter-utils` or a common config module would make this easier to maintain and self-document. The same applies to `server/src/adapters/process/execute.ts`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR wires `countUnreadTouchedByUser()` into the sidebar-badge route so the inbox badge count now includes unread issues, matching what the "Unread" tab displays. The core fix on line 53 is correct — `unreadTouchedIssues` is fetched for `board` actors and added to `badges.inbox` exactly once.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is correct, low-risk, and adds one well-tested DB query per request. All remaining findings are P2 (style/clarity). The functional change is a straightforward one-liner on line 53 with no schema changes, no migration, and no double-counting risk. No files require special attention.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The `countUnreadTouchedByUser` call is gated behind `req.actor.type === "board" && req.actor.userId`, and `companyId` is already validated by `assertCompanyAccess`. No new inputs are accepted from untrusted sources.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/sidebar-badges.ts | Adds `issueService` import and wires `countUnreadTouchedByUser()` into the badge count; the fix is correct — `unreadTouchedIssues` is added once to `badges.inbox` on line 53. Minor redundancy: the value is also passed to `svc.get()` even though `badges.inbox` is immediately overwritten. |

The value fed to `svc.get()` is never surfaced to the caller. Future readers may be confused about whether `svc.get()` or the route is the source of truth for `badges.inbox`. Consider passing only what `svc.get()` uses permanently (i.e. `joinRequests`), or moving the `alertsCount` logic into the service so `badges.inbox` doesn't need to be patched after the fact: ```suggestion const badges = await svc.get(companyId, { joinRequests: joinRequestCount, }); ``` How can I resolve this? 3192.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a bug where managed instruction bundles could still contain legacy `$AGENT_HOME/...` companion links (to `HEARTBEAT.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`) instead of the expected bundle-relative `./...` form. It adds `normalizeManagedBundleFileContent` and `healManagedBundleContent` helpers, hooks them into bundle recovery (`recoverManagedBundleState`), the materialization path (`materializeManagedBundle`), and the bundle-switch path (`writeBundleFiles`/`ensureWritableBundle`), and leaves external bundles untouched. Test coverage for the new behaviour is solid.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; changes are intentionally scoped to managed bundles with solid regression tests. All findings are P2: a hardcoded companion-file list that could miss non-standard references, and a minor write-path inconsistency that self-heals on the next read. Neither causes data loss or incorrect runtime behaviour. The implementation logic is correct, idempotent, and well-tested. server/src/services/agent-instructions.ts — two P2 style points around normalization completeness and write-path consistency

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. Path traversal is guarded by the existing `resolvePathWithinRoot` / `normalizeRelativeFilePath` validators, the new normalization functions only perform string replacement on known literal patterns, and no new trust boundaries or user-controlled code paths are introduced.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/agent-instructions.ts | Core normalization logic added: MANAGED_AGENTS_REFERENCE_REWRITES constant, normalizeManagedBundleFileContent, healManagedBundleContent, and hook into recoverManagedBundleState; writeFile path skips normalization at write time | | server/src/__tests__/agent-instructions-service.test.ts | Adds three regression tests: managed healing, external no-op, and idempotent normalization; tests are well-structured and cover the primary scenarios | | server/src/__tests__/agent-skills-routes.test.ts | Adds route-level test asserting CEO bundle materialization produces normalized ./HEARTBEAT.md / ./SOUL.md / ./TOOLS.md links; properly mocked, no issues |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/services/agent-instructions.ts`, line 659-693 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/9f76feaf2cbe0aca272541668133101d8ac461e6/server/src/services/agent-instructions.ts#L659-L693)) **`writeFile` skips normalization at write time** When a caller explicitly writes to `AGENTS.md` via `svc.writeFile(agent, "AGENTS.md", content)`, the content is persisted verbatim (line 686) without going through `normalizeManagedBundleFileContent`. Normalization does happen on the _next_ `getBundle`/`exportFiles` call via `healManagedBundleContent`, but there's a window between write and the next recovery where the on-disk file contains legacy `$AGENT_HOME/` references. All other write paths (materialization, bundle switch, legacy-instruction bootstrap) normalize eagerly. Applying the same normalization here would keep the write path consistent: ```typescript await fs.writeFile( absolutePath, prepared.state.mode === "managed" ? normalizeManagedBundleFileContent(normalizeRelativeFilePath(relativePath), content) : content, "utf8", ); ```

Normalization does happen on the _next_ `getBundle`/`exportFiles` call via `healManagedBundleContent`, but there's a window between write and the next recovery where the on-disk file contains legacy `$AGENT_HOME/` references. All other write paths (materialization, bundle switch, legacy-instruction bootstrap) normalize eagerly. Applying the same normalization here would keep the write path consistent: ```typescript await fs.writeFile( absolutePath, prepared.state.mode === "managed" ? normalizeManagedBundleFileContent(normalizeRelativeFilePath(relativePath), content) : content, "utf8", ); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3194.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a bug where `tool_use` errors in the OpenCode JSONL stream were being folded into the same `errorMessage` field used for terminal adapter/runtime errors, causing Paperclip to treat recoverable tool failures as fatal run failures. The fix is minimal and correct: tool errors now go into a separate `toolErrors` array exposed as `toolErrorMessages`, while only genuine `type === "error"` events flow into `errorMessage`. The PR also bundles an unrelated feature — healing legacy `$AGENT_HOME/...` references in managed `AGENTS.md` files via `healManagedBundleContent` — which introduces side-effectful file writes on what are otherwise read-only code paths in `agentInstructionsService`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the core fix is correct, well-tested, and isolated to the OpenCode JSONL parser. All remaining findings are P2 (observability and I/O efficiency). The primary fix is straightforward, the logic change in execute.ts flows correctly from the parser change, and both unit and integration-level regression tests pass. No correctness or data-integrity issues found. server/src/services/agent-instructions.ts — the heal-on-read pattern adds I/O to every service call, but introduces no correctness defect.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The parser change only affects error routing in an internal JSONL parser; no user-controlled input reaches new execution paths. The `normalizeManagedBundleFileContent` rewrites are string-literal substitutions on trusted file content within a sandboxed managed root.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/parse.ts | Core fix: tool_use error messages now go into toolErrors/toolErrorMessages instead of the terminal errors/errorMessage, preventing recoverable tool failures from failing entire runs. | | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/parse.test.ts | Adds regression test verifying tool_use errors land in toolErrorMessages with errorMessage null; also adds toolErrorMessages assertion to the existing error-path test. | | server/src/__tests__/opencode-local-adapter.test.ts | Server-level regression test confirming tool_use failures do not surface as terminal adapter errors; also covers session, usage, and cost parsing end-to-end. | | server/src/services/agent-instructions.ts | Adds healManagedBundleContent called on every recoverManagedBundleState, normalizes legacy $AGENT_HOME references; writeBundleFiles now conditionally normalizes content — unrelated to the PR title, and introduces side-effectful writes on read paths. | | server/src/__tests__/agent-instructions-service.test.ts | New test verifies AGENTS.md $AGENT_HOME references are healed in-place while non-entry files (TOOLS.md) are left unchanged. | | server/src/__tests__/agent-skills-routes.test.ts | Test file updated to include the materializeManagedBundle mock, supporting the new agent-instructions service interface. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/parse.ts Line: 87 Comment: **`toolErrorMessages` captured but never forwarded to `AdapterExecutionResult`** `toolErrorMessages` is populated here and tested, but `execute.ts`'s `toResult()` function never reads or forwards it. If any downstream consumer (logging, run metadata, UI) ever needs to inspect which tools failed, the only path is parsing the raw `resultJson.stdout`. That's workable since the raw JSONL is preserved, but consider whether forwarding the array to the result (or to a dedicated field in `resultJson`) would make future debugging easier. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/agent-instructions.ts Line: 361-373 Comment: **`healManagedBundleContent` triggers on every read-path call** `recoverManagedBundleState` is called at the top of every public service method (`getBundle`, `readFile`, `exportFiles`, `deleteFile`, …). Placing a file write (`healManagedBundleContent`) inside it means each read-only operation now potentially mutates the filesystem. The write is gated on a content diff so it's effectively idempotent after the first heal, but it adds an unconditional `fs.readFile` on every invocation even when the file hasn't changed. A one-time migration path (e.g. tracking whether the heal has already run, or running it only on explicit write operations) would avoid the repeated I/O on busy agents. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3196.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a redundant `UPDATE issues SET updatedAt` that `addComment()` was issuing immediately after `svc.update()` had already updated the same row in the PATCH handler, and adds a defensive two-step close-flow instruction for openclaw agents. The server-side fix (`skipUpdatedAt` flag) is correct and clean. One PR-level note: the description is missing the **\"Thinking Path\"** section required by [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) — please add a top-down walkthrough (system → component → problem → fix → benefit → risks) before this merges.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 style/observability notes. The server-side fix is well-scoped and correct — skipUpdatedAt: true is only applied in the PATCH path where svc.update() already touched updatedAt, and standalone POST /comments callers are unaffected. The two-step execute.ts change is a minor behavioural shift (non-atomic close) but the failure window is tiny and the workaround is practical. No P0/P1 issues found. packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts — two-step close flow slightly reduces atomicity; worth a second look if close-flow idempotency is critical.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds optional `skipUpdatedAt` flag to `addComment()` that skips the redundant `UPDATE issues SET updatedAt` when the caller has already updated the issue row; logic is gated cleanly and does not affect standalone comment additions. | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | PATCH handler now passes `{ skipUpdatedAt: true }` when calling `addComment()`, which is correct since `svc.update()` already set `updatedAt` on the same row moments before. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Skill template now instructs agents to POST comment then PATCH status separately; this is a defensive two-step pattern but introduces a non-atomic close flow — comment can be created even if the subsequent PATCH fails. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/routes/issues.ts`, line 1151-1156 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/ea5485fbacbbfc4afc2e706aba46f8202ba260fb/server/src/routes/issues.ts#L1151-L1156)) **`updatedAt` skipped even when PATCH had no field changes** `svc.update()` always emits `UPDATE issues SET updatedAt=now()` — even when `updateFields` contains no meaningful changes (e.g., a PATCH body with only a `comment` field). In that narrow case, passing `skipUpdatedAt: true` to `addComment()` still works correctly because `svc.update()` did set `updatedAt`. However, the inline comment says "Issue already updated above" which is always true regardless of whether any real fields changed, making the assumption a bit implicit. This is low-risk but a small note worth documenting more precisely if this code is referenced later.

3198.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `normalizeOptionalUuidQuery()` helper to validate the `assigneeAgentId` query parameter on `GET /api/companies/{id}/issues`, rejecting non-UUID strings (including the literal `"null"` / `"undefined"`) with a 400 before they reach the DB. Two regression tests cover the invalid and valid-UUID paths.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all findings are P2 style/cleanup suggestions that do not affect correctness. The validation logic is correct: invalid values are rejected before the DB query, and valid UUIDs pass through unchanged. The three inline comments (misleading return type, missing blank line, unvalidated sibling param) are all non-blocking style improvements. No P0/P1 issues found. No files require special attention.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The change is additive validation only; no secrets, injection vectors, or auth bypass introduced.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds UUID validation for `assigneeAgentId` query param; sentinel-string approach and misleading `null` return type are minor style concerns, and `participantAgentId` still lacks equivalent validation. | | server/src/__tests__/issues-route-query-validation.test.ts | New test file covering invalid (`null` string) and valid UUID paths for `assigneeAgentId`; mocks are set up correctly and the happy-path assertion verifies the value reaches the service. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 64-70 Comment: **Misleading return type and `"__invalid__"` sentinel** The declared return type `string | null | undefined` includes `null`, but the function never returns `null`. The caller's `assigneeAgentId ?? undefined` on line 394 is therefore dead code. More importantly, returning the magic string `"__invalid__"` as a sentinel leaks through the `string` branch of the type and could be confused with a real validated value by any future caller. A cleaner design uses a dedicated `false` sentinel (or throws), and drops `null` from the signature: ```suggestion function normalizeOptionalUuidQuery(value: unknown): string | false | undefined { if (typeof value !== "string") return undefined; const trimmed = value.trim(); if (!trimmed) return undefined; if (trimmed === "null" || trimmed === "undefined") return false; return UUID_QUERY_RE.test(trimmed) ? trimmed : false; } ``` Then the call site becomes: ```ts const assigneeAgentId = normalizeOptionalUuidQuery(req.query.assigneeAgentId); if (assigneeAgentId === false) { ... } // assigneeAgentId is now string | undefined — no ?? undefined needed assigneeAgentId: assigneeAgentId, ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 395 Comment: **`participantAgentId` has the same validation gap** `participantAgentId` is still cast directly with `as string | undefined` and passed to `svc.list()` without any UUID validation, the same problem this PR fixes for `assigneeAgentId`. A value like `?participantAgentId=null` will hit the DB unvalidated. Worth applying `normalizeOptionalUuidQuery` here too while the pattern is fresh. ```suggestion participantAgentId: normalizeOptionalUuidQuery(req.query.participantAgentId) || undefined, ``` (and add a corresponding `=== false` guard above, consistent with the `assigneeAgentId` pattern) How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 70-71 Comment: **Missing blank line after function** There is no blank line separating the closing brace of `normalizeOptionalUuidQuery` from the `const updateIssueRouteSchema` declaration, inconsistent with the surrounding style. ```suggestion } const updateIssueRouteSchema = updateIssueSchema.extend({ ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3200.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes orphaned queued heartbeat runs that linger when an issue is reassigned or closed, by adding `cancelQueuedRunsForIssueInternal` to the heartbeat service and wiring it into the issue PATCH handler. The guard conditions and `.catch` wrapping in the route are correct; the main gaps are in the new service function compared to the existing cancellation helpers.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 style/improvement suggestions with no correctness impact. The core fix (cancelling queued runs on reassignment/closure) is correct and guarded. The missing `startNextQueuedRunForAgent` call is a latency gap (agent waits for the next heartbeat cycle) rather than a hard block, and matches the behavior of the existing `cancelActiveForAgentInternal` bulk helper. All open comments are P2. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — the new `cancelQueuedRunsForIssueInternal` diverges from the teardown steps in `cancelRunInternal`.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The `issueId` used in the raw SQL snippet (`contextSnapshot->>'issueId' = ${issueId}`) is passed via Drizzle's parameterized query binding, so no SQL injection risk.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds `cancelQueuedRunsForIssueInternal` — selects queued runs by issueId from contextSnapshot and cancels them. Missing `appendRunEvent` (no lifecycle trace) and `startNextQueuedRunForAgent` (agent not immediately unblocked), and uses `inArray` where `eq` suffices. | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Calls `cancelQueuedRunsForIssue` after a successful PATCH when the issue is reassigned or transitions to done/cancelled. Guard logic and `.catch` wrapping look correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 4279 Comment: **`inArray` with a single-element literal** `inArray(col, ["queued"])` is semantically identical to `eq(col, "queued")` and generates cleaner SQL (`= 'queued'` vs `IN ('queued')`). All other single-status checks in this file use `eq` (e.g. lines 4204, 2478). ```suggestion eq(heartbeatRuns.status, "queued"), ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 4284-4295 Comment: **Agent not unblocked after cancelling its queued runs** After the queued runs are cancelled, `startNextQueuedRunForAgent` is never called. If the agent has other queued work (for different issues), it won't be scheduled to pick it up until the next heartbeat polling cycle. The PR's stated goal is to prevent the agent from being "blocked from picking up new work" — but without this call the agent will still be momentarily stalled rather than immediately resuming. `cancelRunInternal` calls `startNextQueuedRunForAgent(run.agentId)` after every cancellation. Adding the same here (per agent, deduped if needed) would make the fix complete: ```ts for (const run of runs) { await setRunStatus(run.id, "cancelled", { ... }); await setWakeupStatus(run.wakeupRequestId, "cancelled", { ... }); await startNextQueuedRunForAgent(run.agentId); // unblock the agent } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 4284-4295 Comment: **Missing lifecycle run event** `cancelRunInternal` appends a `"run cancelled"` lifecycle event via `appendRunEvent` so the run log shows why the run ended. The new function omits this step, leaving cancelled runs with no diagnostic trail in the event log. Operators inspecting a cancelled run would see no `lifecycle` event explaining it was terminated due to issue reassignment/closure. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3202.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds an "Execution Thread" tab to the Issue Detail page, aggregating activity, comments, status changes, assignments, and blockers across an entire parent-child issue wave into a single chronological timeline. The frontend component, API client, query key, route, and shared types are all cleanly wired together. - **`findRoot` in `execution-thread.ts` (lines 220–236)** issues up to 50 sequential DB round-trips to walk the ancestor chain. With the component polling every 5 seconds, deep trees will generate continuous bursts of sequential queries — this should be replaced with a single recursive CTE before merging. - The remaining findings (service instantiated per-request, silent LIMIT 200 truncation, multi-field update events, docker auto-onboard) are P2 improvements that don't block the feature from shipping.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the N+1 query issue in `findRoot`; all other findings are P2. One P1 finding: `findRoot` makes up to 50 sequential DB queries per request and the component polls every 5 seconds — a real latency and connection-pool risk that should be fixed with a recursive CTE before shipping. All remaining comments are P2 style/quality improvements. `server/src/services/execution-thread.ts` — the `findRoot` function needs to be rewritten as a recursive CTE.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The new `/issues/:id/execution-thread` route correctly gates access with `assertCompanyAccess`. Issue IDs used in the recursive CTE are passed as parameterized SQL values, preventing SQL injection. The `findRoot` traversal does not expose hidden issues in the response.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/execution-thread.ts | New service walking the issue tree and merging timeline entries; `findRoot` makes up to 50 sequential DB round-trips (N+1), and the LIMIT 200 truncation is silent. | | server/src/routes/activity.ts | Adds `GET /issues/:id/execution-thread` route with correct authz; `executionThreadService` is instantiated per-request instead of once at router setup like other services. | | ui/src/components/ExecutionThread.tsx | New polled timeline component; clean structure with deterministic issue badge colors, expandable comment bodies, and per-entry actor/description rendering. | | ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx | Thread tab added conditionally on issues with parent or children; tab and content guards are consistent and existing Activity tab is untouched. | | scripts/docker-entrypoint.sh | Adds targeted ownership fix and auto-onboard on first run; auto-onboard fires unconditionally when config is absent, which may surprise operators using IaC workflows. | | packages/shared/src/types/execution-thread.ts | Well-typed entry types covering all timeline event kinds; exported correctly from shared index. | | ui/src/api/activity.ts | Adds `executionThread` fetch; straightforward and consistent with existing API helpers. | | ui/src/lib/queryKeys.ts | Adds `executionThread` query key under `issues`; follows existing naming and structure. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/execution-thread.ts Line: 220-236 Comment: **N+1 sequential queries in `findRoot`** `findRoot` issues one DB round-trip per ancestor level (up to 50), all in sequence. With the component polling every 5 seconds, deep trees will produce 50 × (requests/s) consecutive queries. This can saturate the connection pool and cause cascading timeouts. Replace the loop with a single recursive CTE — the same pattern already used in `getThread` for the wave. ```suggestion async function findRoot(db: Db, issueId: string): Promise { const rows = await db.execute(sql` WITH RECURSIVE ancestors AS ( SELECT id, "parent_id" AS parent_id FROM issues WHERE id = ${issueId} UNION ALL SELECT i.id, i."parent_id" AS parent_id FROM issues i INNER JOIN ancestors a ON i.id = a.parent_id ) SELECT id FROM ancestors WHERE parent_id IS NULL LIMIT 1 `) as unknown as Array<{ id: string }>; return rows[0]?.id ?? issueId; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/activity.ts Line: 82-93 Comment: **Service instantiated per request** `executionThreadService(db)` is called inside the route handler, creating a new service instance on every request. Every other service in this router (`activityService`, `issueService`) is instantiated once at setup time on lines 23–24. For consistency, add `const threadSvc = executionThreadService(db);` alongside the other service declarations at the top of `activityRoutes` and reference it in the handler. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/execution-thread.ts Line: 40 Comment: **Silent truncation at 200 issues with no UI indicator** When a wave exceeds 200 issues, results are silently cut off. Neither the API response nor the UI communicates that the timeline is incomplete. Consider including a `truncated: boolean` flag (or `totalIssueCount`) in `ExecutionThreadResponse` so the frontend can show a notice like "Showing first 200 of N issues". How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/execution-thread.ts Line: 94-141 Comment: **`issue.updated` events that change both `status` and another field drop the non-status change** When an update event contains `status` alongside other generic fields (e.g. `title`, `description`), the `status_change` entry is emitted but the generic-update branch (`!("status" in details) && ...`) never fires, so the other changed fields are silently ignored. The same event payload can cover multiple fields. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/docker-entrypoint.sh Line: 35-44 Comment: **Auto-onboard on first run may be unexpected in production** The new block silently runs `pnpm paperclipai onboard --yes` when no config file exists. Operators who intentionally defer configuration (e.g., IaC workflows that write config before starting the container) will get an auto-generated default config that could conflict with their setup. A guard or opt-in env var (e.g. `PAPERCLIP_AUTO_ONBOARD=1`) would make this safer. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3211.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds auto-scroll-to-bottom behaviour to the `embedded` variant of `IssueChatThread`, with a passive scroll listener that detects when the user has scrolled up and pauses auto-scroll accordingly. - **P1 – TypeScript compilation error**: the cleanup closure captures `el` (a `let HTMLElement | null`) without assigning it to a `const` first; TypeScript widens it back to `HTMLElement | null` inside closures, producing an \"Object is possibly 'null'\" error under `strict` mode. Fix by using a `const foundEl = el` before closing over it. - The PR checklist notes that no local tests were run and no before/after screenshots were provided — please add screenshots per contribution guidelines.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until the TypeScript closure null-safety error is fixed; otherwise the build will fail under strict mode. One P1 finding (closure captures a `let HTMLElement | null` without a const intermediate, causing a strict-mode TypeScript compile error) blocks merge. The remaining finding (hash-anchor race) is a P2 edge case. Once the const fix is applied the PR is a clean, well-scoped change. ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.tsx — specifically the scroll container useEffect cleanup closure around line 1741.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The change only wires up a passive DOM scroll listener and calls `scrollIntoView` — no user input is processed, no data is transmitted, and no auth boundaries are touched.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.tsx | Adds auto-scroll logic for the embedded variant; has a TypeScript closure null-safety error (let vs const for scroll container) that will fail strict-mode compilation, and a minor race with the existing hash-anchor scroll on initial render. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.tsx Line: 1741-1743 Comment: **TypeScript null-safety error in cleanup closure** `el` is declared as `let el: HTMLElement | null`, and TypeScript's control-flow narrowing does **not** carry across closure boundaries for mutable `let` variables. When the cleanup function is later called, TypeScript widens `el` back to `HTMLElement | null`, so `el.removeEventListener(...)` will produce a "Object is possibly 'null'" compile error under `strict` mode. Since the PR author has not run local type-checks (checklist item unchecked), this would surface at build time. Assign the found element to a `const` before closing over it: ```suggestion const foundEl = el; scrollContainerRef.current = foundEl; foundEl.addEventListener("scroll", handleScroll, { passive: true }); return () => { foundEl.removeEventListener("scroll", handleScroll); }; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.tsx Line: 1749-1753 Comment: **Auto-scroll fires on mount even when pre-loaded with a hash anchor** The existing hash-scroll effect (line ~1709) scrolls to a specific `#comment-xxx` element once on first render. Because this new auto-scroll effect also fires on every `messages` change (including the initial mount) and runs after all effects, it will win the race and scroll back to the bottom, silently defeating the hash-scroll for any URL with a `#comment-` / `#activity-` / `#run-` fragment. The embedded variant is less likely to be deep-linked today, but the interaction is non-obvious. Consider skipping the initial auto-scroll when a relevant hash is present: ```suggestion useEffect(() => { if (variant !== "embedded") return; if (userScrolledUpRef.current) return; // Don't override an intentional hash-based scroll on first render const hash = location.hash; if (!hasScrolledRef.current && (hash.startsWith("#comment-") || hash.startsWith("#activity-") || hash.startsWith("#run-"))) return; bottomAnchorRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "end" }); }, [variant, messages, location.hash]); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3212.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR updates the hardcoded SHA-256 hash in the `Dockerfile` for the GitHub CLI signing keyring (`githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg`), which was rotated on April 8, 2026 (see [cli/cli#13118](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/13118)), fixing Docker builds that were previously failing at the `sha256sum -c` verification step. The PR description doesn't follow the required PR template from [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) and `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`. All of the following sections are required before this can be merged: - **Thinking Path** — trace from project context down to this specific fix - **What Changed** — bullet list of concrete changes - **Verification** — how a reviewer can confirm this works (e.g., `docker build` succeeding) - **Risks** — assessment of risk (e.g., \"Low risk — hash-only update for a rotated upstream key\") - **Model Used** — AI model used, or \"None — human-authored\" - **Checklist** — all boxes checked

Confidence Score: 4/5

Unblocking fix with a trivial one-line change, but the new hash should be confirmed against an official source and the PR template must be filled out before merging. The code change is correct and necessary — Docker builds were broken by the upstream key rotation. The only gaps are: (1) the new hash wasn't traced to an official canonical source in the PR, and (2) the PR is missing all required template sections (Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) per CONTRIBUTING.md. These are process requirements that should be satisfied before merge. Dockerfile — verify the new SHA-256 hash against the official GitHub CLI release announcement.

Vulnerabilities

 - The SHA-256 verification step itself is a sound security control — it pins the downloaded signing key to a known-good value, preventing use of a tampered keyring. Confirming the new hash against an authoritative source (official GitHub CLI announcement or install docs) would close the remaining gap before merging.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | Dockerfile | Updates the SHA-256 hash for the GitHub CLI signing key to match the newly rotated keyring (referenced in cli/cli#13118); the new hash should be verified against official release notes. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: Dockerfile Line: 8 Comment: **Consider sourcing the hash from official release documentation** The new SHA-256 value `6084d5d7bd8e288441e0e94fc6275570895da18e6751f70f057485dc2d1a811b` appears to come from the newly rotated keyring, but it would help to confirm this against the official GitHub CLI announcement (e.g., the [#13118 issue thread](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/13118) or their Linux install docs). If the hash was derived by running the failed build and capturing the downloaded file's hash, it's technically correct but unverified against an authoritative source. Adding a comment citing where the hash came from would help future maintainers audit this quickly. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3214.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR restricts `generate_calendar` in the Social Agent to a single Thursday 8am–noon CT window (only when next week's calendar is completely absent), and bans speculative `generate_video`/`generate_image` calls — directly addressing runaway daily Runway charges traced to heartbeat-triggered calendar regeneration. The PR description is missing the "thinking path" format required by CONTRIBUTING.md, along with verification steps and a risk section. Please add those to the description so the PR meets the project's contribution guidelines.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all findings are P2 quality/process suggestions that don't block correctness. The core logic in social-agent.md clearly and correctly encodes the intended cost discipline rules. All review findings are P2: the missing thinking path is a process issue, the hardcoded CLAW_ROOT is a portability nit, and the heartbeat drift is a design suggestion rather than a current defect. No P0/P1 issues found. agents/instructions/social-agent.md (Thursday window timing), scripts/seed-truwitz.ts (hardcoded CLAW_ROOT path)

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The changes are limited to agent instruction files and a seed script; no auth logic, secret handling, or data exposure paths were introduced.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | agents/instructions/social-agent.md | Core change: adds Thursday 8am–noon CT gate and "absent calendar only" guard for generate_calendar; no explicit time-check step and heartbeat drift could cause the window to be missed. | | scripts/seed-truwitz.ts | Seeds Truwitz company and agents; CLAW_ROOT is hardcoded to an absolute path on one developer's machine, breaking portability. | | agents/instructions/brand-lead.md | Brand Lead instructions; no changes with significant issues detected. | | agents/instructions/chief-of-staff.md | Chief of Staff instructions; no significant issues in the changed content. | | agents/instructions/lead-gen-agent.md | Lead Gen Agent instructions; no significant issues detected. | | agents/instructions/ops-agent.md | Ops Agent instructions; no significant issues detected. | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Heartbeat service; no issues specific to this PR's changes identified. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: agents/instructions/social-agent.md Line: 9-11 Comment: **No explicit step to verify current time/timezone** The instructions require checking whether it is "Thursday between 8 AM and noon Central Time", but the workflow has no dedicated step to confirm the current date and time before branching. The `Tools Available` section lists only `claw-social` and `slack` — neither exposes a time tool. The agent will likely fall back to running `date` in bash, but that's implicit and could be skipped or misread. Consider adding an explicit first step like *"Use `date` (bash) to capture the current day of week and hour in CT (`TZ=America/Chicago date`) before making any calendar decisions"* so the guard is unambiguous. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/seed-truwitz.ts Line: 21 Comment: **Hardcoded absolute path tied to a single developer's machine** `CLAW_ROOT` is hardcoded to `/Users/nathanstewart/projects/claw`. Anyone else running this seed script (or a CI pipeline) will produce agents whose `cwd` points at a non-existent path, causing every heartbeat to fail at startup. Consider reading this from an environment variable (e.g. `process.env.CLAW_ROOT`) or documenting that it must be set before running. ```suggestion const CLAW_ROOT = process.env.CLAW_ROOT ?? "/Users/nathanstewart/projects/claw"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: agents/instructions/social-agent.md Line: 9-13 Comment: **Thursday window may be missed due to heartbeat drift** The social agents are seeded with `intervalSec: 86400` (24 h). Because the interval is measured from when the *previous run finished*, each run's actual wall-clock fire time drifts by its own duration. Over weeks this accumulates, and the Thursday heartbeat can silently drift past the 8 AM–noon CT window. If that happens, `generate_calendar` is never called that week (by design — the instructions say not to self-heal outside the window), and the brand has no content calendar. Consider either: - Changing the Thursday social agents to a smaller interval (e.g. hourly on Thu) so they're guaranteed to fire inside the window, or - Using a cron-based schedule instead of a fixed interval to pin the fire time to a specific Thursday slot. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `CONFLICT` · severity: `high` · commit: `b8b4b5fe` > [!WARNING] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary The PR title is "Tighten social-agent cost discipline." The actual diff is **666 additions across 7 files**, breaking down as: 1. **488 LOC of company-specific seed data and agent prompts** (`agents/instructions/brand-lead.md`, `chief-of-staff.md`, `lead-gen-agent.md`, `ops-agent.md`, `social-agent.md`, and `scripts/seed-truwitz.ts`) — a complete agent roster + seed script for a specific company called "truwitz". 2. **Two major undocumented core changes in `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts`**: - **Global 15-minute heartbeat execution timeout** (`HEARTBEAT_EXECUTION_TIMEOUT_MS`) - **Per-agent circuit breaker with exponential backoff** (`CB_FAILURE_THRESHOLD`, `CB_MAX_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER`, `CircuitBreakerState` stored in `agentRuntimeState.stateJson`) Neither core change is mentioned in the PR title or summary, and both are policy decisions that affect every Paperclip deployment, not just the social agent. Two distinct tree-conflict concerns below.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Company-specific seed data in core | `seed-truwitz.ts` + 5 truwitz agent instruction files in main repo | [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) **Anti-requirements**: "Paperclip is explicitly **not**: an agent runtime, a knowledge base, **... or a complete Jira/GitHub replacement**" + "Paperclip is infrastructure, **not the company itself**" | ⚠️ Conflict | | Hidden core policy: 15min heartbeat timeout | Adds a global execution timeout cap not mentioned in title/summary | [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) compaction thresholds reference suggests timeout policy is core behavior worth declaring on the tree | ⚠️ Conflict — undocumented policy change | | Hidden core policy: per-agent circuit breaker | Exponential heartbeat backoff after 3 consecutive failures | [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery." — automatic backoff IS auto-recovery | ⚠️ Conflict |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — anti-requirements, control plane vs company - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" / no silent auto-recovery - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter timeout pattern ### Recommendation This PR should be **split into three** before merging, in this order: 1. **A separate "core: per-agent circuit breaker for heartbeat failures" PR** that explicitly proposes the auto-backoff policy. This is a governance-relevant decision (auto-recovery), needs a tree update before it lands, and absolutely should not ship hidden inside a "tighten social-agent" PR. The 15-minute global heartbeat timeout decision belongs in the same PR. 2. **A separate "scripts: truwitz company seed" PR** in a `examples/` or `seeds/` directory if the maintainer is OK with example seeds in the core repo at all. **My read of [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) is that this content does not belong in `paperclipai/paperclip` at all** — Paperclip is the control plane, not the company. Truwitz should be its own repo using Paperclip as a dependency, with seed data living there. 3. **The actual social-agent calendar throttle** (whatever code change the title describes) — but I don't see a corresponding code change in the diff. The 666 LOC are all seeds + instructions + heartbeat policy. Either the calendar throttle is missing or it's hidden inside one of the markdown instruction files. If it's in a `.md` instruction file, that's a documentation/prompt update, not a code change, and the PR title overstates what it does. Pulling this apart will let the governance-affecting parts get the review they deserve and keep the core repo from accreting company-specific seeds. --- Reviewed commit: b8b4b5fe · Tree snapshot: ffda000b · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `low` · commit: `14be2da1` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. 3218.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces `killOrphanedPid` — a SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL helper — and wires it into `cancelRunInternal`, `cancelActiveForAgentInternal`, and `reapOrphanedRuns` so that agent processes that survive a server restart are forcibly terminated rather than left running indefinitely. The change is well-scoped and the updated tests correctly cover the alive-orphan path. The PR description is missing several required sections from the PR template (`CONTRIBUTING.md`): a **Thinking Path**, a **Model Used** section (with provider, model ID, and version), a **Risks** section, and the Checklist. Please fill those in before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the PR template gap; the code logic itself is sound. All code findings are P2. The one item that lowers the score from 5 is the missing required PR template sections (Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks, Checklist) mandated by CONTRIBUTING.md — a process requirement that must be satisfied before merge per repo policy. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — minor: SIGKILL timer should call .unref()

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The PID-reuse caveat on Linux (documented in the `isProcessAlive` comment) is a known OS-level limitation, not a new vulnerability introduced by this PR.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds `killOrphanedPid` helper (SIGTERM → SIGKILL after 5 s grace) and wires it into `cancelRunInternal`, `cancelActiveForAgentInternal`, and `reapOrphanedRuns`; the SIGKILL timer is not unref'd, which can hold the event loop open during server shutdown. | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-process-recovery.test.ts | Updates heartbeat recovery tests to assert that an alive orphaned process is killed and its run is marked `failed`; test coverage for all five scenarios looks correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 1029-1035 Comment: **Timer should be unref'd to avoid blocking shutdown** The `setTimeout` callback holds the Node.js event loop open for up to 5 s. If the server begins a graceful shutdown immediately after `killOrphanedPid` fires (e.g. during the same reaper cycle), the process won't exit cleanly until every pending timer has drained. Calling `.unref()` lets the event loop exit without waiting for the SIGKILL guard to fire if nothing else is keeping it alive. ```suggestion setTimeout(() => { if (isProcessAlive(numPid)) { try { process.kill(numPid, "SIGKILL"); } catch { /* already gone */ } } }, graceMs).unref(); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary Closes #3215. Adds `killOrphanedPid()` (SIGTERM → SIGKILL after 5s grace), wires it as a fallback in `cancelRunInternal` and `cancelActiveForAgentInternal` when the in-memory `ChildProcess` handle is gone, and changes the stale-run reaper to actually kill orphaned processes and mark runs `failed`. This is the **third PR in the queue** addressing the orphaned-execution-cleanup pattern (after #3200 cancels orphaned queued runs on reassign/close, and #3190 clears stale `executionRunId` lock metadata). The trio is internally consistent and complementary, but they should be coordinated.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Reaper kills, doesn't observe | Stale-run reaper now SIGKILLs orphans and marks runs failed | [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) principle 6: "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery." — but a documented timeout-based reaper is closer to "surfacing a problem as a failure" than to "hiding it" | ❓ Partial | | SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL grace | 5s grace period before hard kill | Standard process-management pattern | ✅ Aligned | | Trigger | Reaper runs on a schedule (background); kill is unattended | Tree hasn't drawn the line on whether scheduled cleanup is "auto-recovery" | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" - [`engineering/backend/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — heartbeat path - (Suggested new) `product/task-system/NODE.md` — explicit auto-cleanup boundary ### Recommendation This is the third PR I've flagged on the same auto-cleanup-vs-auto-recovery question (#3200, #3190, #3218). The boundary needs to be drawn explicitly in the tree before more PRs in this category arrive. 1. **Coordinate with #3200 and #3190.** All three PRs touch the orphaned-run cleanup path: - #3200 — cancel queued runs when issue is reassigned/closed (event-triggered) - #3190 — clear stale `executionRunId` metadata on issue state transitions (event-triggered) - #3218 — SIGKILL orphaned PIDs in scheduled reaper (background-triggered) The first two are gated on explicit human/agent state changes. **#3218 is the first one that's purely background-driven** — that's a meaningful difference for the "no silent auto-recovery" principle and worth a maintainer decision. 2. **The stale-run reaper marking runs `failed` is a behavior change** worth flagging in the changelog. Operators monitoring run status will see `failed` runs that previously appeared as `running`-with-warning-log. 3. **Add a tree node.** `product/task-system/NODE.md` should codify when scheduled cleanup is allowed vs. forbidden. This category of PR will keep arriving until the tree has an opinion. Otherwise the implementation is well-scoped and tested. --- Reviewed commit: e733cee0 · Tree snapshot: ffda000b · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore

The "Auto" model fallback — automatically switching models when one hits a rate limit — touches the adapter boundary design and the "surface problems, don't hide them" principle, and warrants more discussion.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Gemini 3.5 model support | Add Gemini 3.5 to the model selection dropdown | Adapters expose `detectModel` and model listing; updating the Gemini model list when new versions ship is routine adapter maintenance | ✅ Aligned | | Auto model fallback on rate limit | When "Auto" is selected and the current model hits quota, silently switch to another model | Tree says no quota reporting exists for the Gemini adapter ("Google's Gemini API does not expose rate-limit windows in the same way"). 3230.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a self-hosted MinIO S3 storage layer to the docker-compose stack, wires the required AWS env vars to the server container, and introduces a new `aws-file-store` skill that lets agents use S3 as a persistent knowledge base via the AWS CLI. The PR description does not follow the required PR template from `CONTRIBUTING.md` — it is missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and the filled-out **Checklist**. Please copy the template from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`, fill in all sections, and include manual testing notes to verify the MinIO bucket init flow works end-to-end.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is completed per the template; all code-level findings are P2 suggestions. All inline findings are P2 (style/best-practice suggestions). The most important blocker is the incomplete PR description, which is a process requirement rather than a code defect. Dockerfile (SHA256 verification), docker/docker-compose.yml (image pinning, endpoint override), skills/aws-file-store/SKILL.md (alwaysApply scope)

Vulnerabilities

 - The server container is configured with MinIO root credentials (`MINIO_ROOT_USER` / `MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD`) as its S3 access keys. For production hardening, a dedicated MinIO service account with bucket-scoped permissions should be used instead of the root account. - The GitHub CLI keyring SHA256 was changed without explanation; this should be independently verified against the file at `https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg` to rule out supply-chain tampering. - No other security concerns identified.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | Dockerfile | Updates GitHub CLI keyring SHA256 hash; the new hash should be verified against the official source before merging | | docker/docker-compose.yml | Adds MinIO service, bucket-init sidecar, and wires S3 env vars to server; uses `latest` image tags and hardcodes `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` without an override path | | skills/aws-file-store/SKILL.md | New skill for S3-backed knowledge base via AWS CLI; `alwaysApply: true` means the skill is injected into every agent context even when S3 is unconfigured | | skills/aws-file-store/references/commands.md | Extended command reference for the aws-file-store skill; no issues found |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: docker/docker-compose.yml Line: 19 Comment: **Pin image tags to avoid unexpected upgrades** Both `minio/minio:latest` and `minio/mc:latest` (line 37) use floating `latest` tags. If MinIO releases a breaking change or a new image is pulled on a fresh deploy, the setup could silently break. Pin to a specific version (e.g., `minio/minio:RELEASE.2025-04-08T15-41-24Z`) for reproducible deployments. ```suggestion image: minio/minio:RELEASE.2025-04-08T15-41-24Z ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: docker/docker-compose.yml Line: 66 Comment: **`AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` is not overridable** `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` is hardcoded to `http://minio:9000` while all other S3-related values accept env-var overrides. Any operator who wants to swap MinIO for real AWS S3 (or a different endpoint) would need to edit the compose file directly. Consider following the same pattern as the other vars: ```suggestion AWS_ENDPOINT_URL: "${AWS_ENDPOINT_URL:-http://minio:9000}" ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: skills/aws-file-store/SKILL.md Line: 2 Comment: **`alwaysApply: true` injects the skill into every agent regardless of S3 configuration** With `alwaysApply: true`, this skill is included in every agent's context even when `FILE_STORE_BUCKET`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, etc. are not set. Agents will follow the MANDATORY rules and attempt S3 operations that will silently fail or produce noisy errors in deployments that haven't configured S3. Consider setting `alwaysApply: false` and having the orchestrator enable the skill only when the required env vars are present, or at minimum add a guard in the MANDATORY rules section noting that agents should first verify reachability via the HEALTH CHECK before attempting any operations. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: Dockerfile Line: 8 Comment: **Verify the updated SHA256 against the official source** The keyring SHA256 changed from `20e0125d…` to `6084d5d7…`. The PR description doesn't explain what triggered this update (e.g., a new GitHub CLI release rotating the signing key). Please confirm the new hash matches the file currently served at `https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg` — a mismatch here would either break the build or, if the hash was derived from a tampered file, introduce a supply-chain risk. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary This PR adds a MinIO-backed shared knowledge base as infrastructure (docker-compose service + injected skill) and wires AWS S3 env vars directly into the server. The context tree explicitly classifies knowledge bases as out-of-scope for the core control plane and deferred from V1. Knowledge bases belong in plugins, not the core. | ⚠️ Conflict | | V1 scope | Ship knowledge base capability now | `product/NODE.md` V1 Scope: "Explicitly deferred from V1: plugin framework/SDK, knowledge base" | ⚠️ Conflict | | Core vs plugin boundary | New MinIO service wired directly into docker-compose server block | `product/NODE.md` "Thin core, rich edges — knowledge bases… go into plugins, not the core control plane." `plugins/NODE.md` defines the correct extension point. | ⚠️ Conflict | | `alwaysApply: true` skill | Skill injected into every agent session regardless of company config | Tree governance principle: company-scoped isolation; capabilities should be opt-in per company | ❓ Partial | | Dockerfile SHA update | Update GitHub CLI keyring hash | Routine maintenance, no tree impact | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Anti-requirements ("not a knowledge base"), thin-core principle, V1 scope defers knowledge base, "knowledge bases go into plugins" - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin SDK + runtime as the correct extension boundary for capabilities like a knowledge base - [`infrastructure/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — docker-compose is core deployment config; adding MinIO here embeds the knowledge base into every deployment
### Recommendation Do not merge as-is. Two paths forward: 1. **Defer** until after V1 ships and the plugin SDK is stable, then build this as a first-party plugin (e.g. `@paperclipai/plugin-file-store`) using the plugin SDK's process isolation and capability gates — exactly what the tree says knowledge-base features should use. 2. **Discuss** with the team whether this decision should be revisited in the tree. If the project's stance on knowledge-base-in-core has changed, update `product/NODE.md` first, then re-open this PR with the tree as backing. The Dockerfile SHA bump in this PR is safe; it can be cherry-picked to a separate PR if the team wants that fix now without the knowledge-base feature. --- Reviewed commit: d1815b0 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3231.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR addresses a production thundering-herd problem where 55 concurrent agents all trigger `opencode models` discovery simultaneously, causing cascading heartbeat failures. The fix spans two areas: the opencode adapter gets a 5-minute model cache, a 45s discovery timeout, and 2-retry exponential backoff; the heartbeat scheduler gets deterministic per-agent jitter (0–10% of interval, SHA-256 seeded) and a Fisher-Yates shuffle to spread lock contention. The PR description does not follow the required PR template from CONTRIBUTING.md — it is missing the **Thinking Path**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Please fill out the template fully before merging.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated with the required template sections; all remaining code findings are P2 style suggestions. The core logic (cache TTL, retry backoff, deterministic jitter, shuffle) is correctly implemented and directly addresses the stated production failure mode. The two inline comments are both P2 — one is an optional single-flight optimization, the other is a minor behavioral note about unidirectional jitter. Neither blocks correctness or safety. packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/models.ts — concurrent cache-miss stampede worth addressing before heavy load returns.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The SHA-256 usage is for non-security-sensitive deterministic hashing (jitter seeding), and the cache key hashes environment variable values before storing them, which is appropriate.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/models.ts | Adds 5-min cache TTL, 45s discovery timeout, and 2-retry exponential backoff; implementation is solid but the cache lacks single-flight deduplication, meaning a burst of concurrent cache misses can still stampede. | | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/models.test.ts | Test timeouts updated to 30s to cover retry backoff delays; existing test coverage is adequate for the changed paths. | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds deterministic per-agent heartbeat jitter via SHA-256 hash and Fisher-Yates shuffle; logic is correct, though jitter is always additive (agents fire 0–10% late rather than ±5% around the interval). |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/models.ts`, line 178-195 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/7c941fe5e11ae3ad9056025ab63b08fce8f2915e/packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/models.ts#L178-L195)) **Concurrent cache-miss stampede not prevented** `discoverOpenCodeModelsCached` checks the cache and, on a miss, immediately calls `discoverOpenCodeModels` — but it doesn't hold a lock or in-flight promise while discovery runs. If 55 agents simultaneously hit the same cache key during a miss (e.g., right after a 5-minute TTL expiry), all 55 will concurrently spawn up to 3 `opencode models` processes each (up to 165 total), recreating the thundering-herd the PR intends to fix. A single-flight map keyed on the cache key would prevent duplicate in-flight work: ```ts const inflightDiscovery = new Map>(); export async function discoverOpenCodeModelsCached(...): Promise { // ... existing cache hit check ... const inflight = inflightDiscovery.get(key); if (inflight) return inflight; const promise = discoverOpenCodeModels({ command, cwd, env }).then((models) => { discoveryCache.set(key, { expiresAt: Date.now() + MODELS_CACHE_TTL_MS, models }); inflightDiscovery.delete(key); return models; }, (err) => { inflightDiscovery.delete(key); throw err; }); inflightDiscovery.set(key, promise); return promise; } ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/models.ts Line: 178-195 Comment: **Concurrent cache-miss stampede not prevented** `discoverOpenCodeModelsCached` checks the cache and, on a miss, immediately calls `discoverOpenCodeModels` — but it doesn't hold a lock or in-flight promise while discovery runs. If 55 agents simultaneously hit the same cache key during a miss (e.g., right after a 5-minute TTL expiry), all 55 will concurrently spawn up to 3 `opencode models` processes each (up to 165 total), recreating the thundering-herd the PR intends to fix. A single-flight map keyed on the cache key would prevent duplicate in-flight work: ```ts const inflightDiscovery = new Map>(); export async function discoverOpenCodeModelsCached(...): Promise { // ... existing cache hit check ... const inflight = inflightDiscovery.get(key); if (inflight) return inflight; const promise = discoverOpenCodeModels({ command, cwd, env }).then((models) => { discoveryCache.set(key, { expiresAt: Date.now() + MODELS_CACHE_TTL_MS, models }); inflightDiscovery.delete(key); return models; }, (err) => { inflightDiscovery.delete(key); throw err; }); inflightDiscovery.set(key, promise); return promise; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

If 55 agents simultaneously hit the same cache key during a miss (e.g., right after a 5-minute TTL expiry), all 55 will concurrently spawn up to 3 `opencode models` processes each (up to 165 total), recreating the thundering-herd the PR intends to fix. A single-flight map keyed on the cache key would prevent duplicate in-flight work: ```ts const inflightDiscovery = new Map>(); export async function discoverOpenCodeModelsCached(...): Promise { // ... existing cache hit check ... const inflight = inflightDiscovery.get(key); if (inflight) return inflight; const promise = discoverOpenCodeModels({ command, cwd, env }).then((models) => { discoveryCache.set(key, { expiresAt: Date.now() + MODELS_CACHE_TTL_MS, models }); inflightDiscovery.delete(key); return models; }, (err) => { inflightDiscovery.delete(key); throw err; }); inflightDiscovery.set(key, promise); return promise; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 87-90 Comment: **Jitter only extends the heartbeat interval (never shortens it)** `jitterFraction` is always in `[0, 1]`, so `jitterMs` is always ≥ 0. Every agent fires *at or after* `intervalSec + jitter`, never earlier. For an agent whose `jitterFraction ≈ 1` this adds a consistent 10% latency to every heartbeat cycle. If the interval is tight (e.g. 30 s), the agent always runs at ~33 s, which could affect latency-sensitive tasks. Consider symmetric jitter (±5%) so the average stays at `intervalSec`: ```ts const jitterMs = Math.floor( policy.intervalSec * 1000 * 0.10 * (jitterFraction - 0.5), ); // range: -5% to +5% of interval ``` This isn't a blocking issue given the production context, but it's worth noting as a behavior change where agents will consistently fire slightly behind their nominal interval. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3232.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a persistence bug where clearing the last environment variable binding in the agent config form had no effect — the server's merge-style PATCH retained the old `env` value because the cleared field was `undefined` and thus omitted from the JSON payload. The fix introduces `buildAgentUpdatePatch` in `ui/src/lib/agent-config-patch.ts`, which builds a full-replacement payload (`replaceAdapterConfig: true`) and strips `undefined` entries with `omitUndefinedEntries` so explicit clears are propagated correctly. `AgentConfigForm` now delegates save payload construction to this helper, and two Vitest regressions cover the clearing and adapter-type-change cases.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — targeted fix with correct logic, full test coverage for the regression, and no P0/P1 findings. All three changed files are clean: the helper correctly strips undefined entries and sets replaceAdapterConfig: true, the adapter-type-change path carries the right agnostic keys from the existing config, and both regression tests assert the correct end-to-end payloads. No critical or warning-level issues were found. No files require special attention.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The change is client-side payload construction only; it serialises data already entered by an authenticated user and relies on the existing server-side `replaceAdapterConfig` path, introducing no new input-validation or injection surface.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/lib/agent-config-patch.ts | New helper that builds the agent update payload with full-replacement semantics and strips undefined entries; logic is clean and correct for both normal saves and adapter-type changes. | | ui/src/lib/agent-config-patch.test.ts | Two focused regression tests covering the cleared-env and adapter-type-change scenarios; test factory helpers are clean and the assertions are complete. | | ui/src/components/AgentConfigForm.tsx | Switched save payload construction to buildAgentUpdatePatch; adapter-type-change handler still seeds overlay.adapterConfig with adapter-specific defaults, while agnostic keys are preserved by the helper. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/lib/agent-config-patch.ts Line: 11-19 Comment: **Duplicate `ADAPTER_AGNOSTIC_KEYS` list can drift from the server** `server/src/routes/agents.ts` defines its own `ADAPTER_AGNOSTIC_KEYS` as a fallback for merge-style patches: ``` ["env", "cwd", "timeoutSec", "graceSec", "promptTemplate", "bootstrapPromptTemplate"] ``` This client-side list adds `"instructionsFilePath"` (handled separately on the server via `preserveInstructionsBundleConfig`) and omits nothing. Because the client now owns the full-replacement payload, the client list is the authoritative source — but there is no enforcement linking the two. If a future adapter-agnostic key is added to the server list but not here (or vice versa), it will be silently dropped on every full-replacement save. A shared constant exported from `@paperclipai/shared` or a cross-reference comment would make the relationship explicit. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3234.body:
Greptile Summary

Updates the hardcoded SHA256 checksum for `githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg` in the Dockerfile from the pre-rotation value to `6084d5d7...`, restoring Docker builds broken by GitHub's confirmed PGP key rotation on April 8, 2026 ([cli/cli#13118](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/13118)). The fix is correct and the sha256sum format is valid; a P2 suggestion exists to remove or parameterise the pinned hash to avoid future pipeline breakage on the next rotation.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — this is a targeted infrastructure fix for a confirmed, publicly documented key rotation event. The only finding is a P2 style suggestion to remove the hardcoded hash to prevent future breakage. The change itself is correct: the key rotation is confirmed by GitHub's own issue tracker, the new hash format is valid (64 hex chars), and the sha256sum syntax is correct. No logic, security, or correctness issues are present. No files require special attention.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The updated hash maintains supply-chain integrity verification for the GitHub CLI GPG keyring. The `signed-by=` apt source configuration already establishes the trust anchor, so the integrity check is defence-in-depth rather than the sole security control.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | Dockerfile | Updates SHA256 checksum for GitHub CLI GPG keyring from the pre-rotation hash to `6084d5d7...` following GitHub's confirmed April 8, 2026 key rotation (cli/cli#13118); format and two-space sha256sum syntax are correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: Dockerfile Line: 8 Comment: **Hardcoded hash is fragile across key rotations** This PR itself is evidence of the problem: GitHub has now rotated their signing key three times (2022, 2024, and April 8, 2026 per [cli/cli#13118](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/13118)), each time requiring a manual Dockerfile update and breaking the build. The pinned hash check adds friction without adding meaningful security — the trust anchor is already the `signed-by=` reference in the apt sources list, and the download uses HTTPS. Consider removing the hash pin entirely, or parameterising it as a build arg so future rotations don't block the pipeline: ```suggestion && wget -nv -O/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \ ``` If the security team requires a pinned hash, document the update procedure in a comment so the next rotation can be handled quickly without a broken build. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Multi-machine agent coordination | Run a "slave" Paperclip on Mac so agents can build Mac targets | Tree describes Paperclip as a control plane that agents phone home to; multi-instance federation or remote agent dispatch is not addressed | ❔ Insufficient | | Local-first, cloud-ready | User has a local Windows instance and wants to extend to Mac | Tree principle supports local deployment; cross-machine orchestration would require network-connected instances but no design exists for this | ❔ Insufficient | | Adapter abstraction | Agents run wherever they run and report back via adapters | An agent running on a Mac machine could theoretically connect back to the Windows Paperclip via the OpenClaw gateway pattern, but this isn't documented as a supported deployment | ❔ Insufficient |
