### Recommendation This looks primarily like a user support question rather than a feature request. Maintainers should clarify: 1. Is the user asking whether Paperclip supports multi-machine deployments where agents on a remote Mac phone home to a Windows control plane? (If so, the OpenClaw gateway is potentially the right path.) 2. Or is this purely a build tooling question outside Paperclip's scope? **🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · issue: `#3236` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary The board inbox filtering request is partially aligned with Paperclip's board-level UX goals and progressive disclosure principle, but one key ask — hiding agent run failures from the board inbox — sits in tension with the "surface problems, don't hide them" core principle. The exact filtering design requires a maintainer judgment call.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Board UX / actionable inbox | Separate actionable items (approvals, escalations, in_review) from operational noise | Tree says the board experience should answer "what needs my approval" — filtering towards board-relevant actions is directionally sound | ✅ Aligned | | Progressive disclosure | Move agent operational noise to a separate tab/view | Tree principle: top layer = human-readable summary, bottom layer = raw logs. Separating board-action items from agent run logs aligns with this layering | ✅ Aligned | | Surface problems, don't hide them | Request asks to hide agent run failures and heartbeat errors from the board inbox entirely | Tree is explicit: no silent auto-recovery, no hiding of failures. Board must be able to see what's broken. Hiding failures in a separate tab is one thing; disabling them entirely risks obscuring real problems | ❓ Partial | | Governance board powers | Board retains unrestricted access to pause, terminate, and override any agent | Filtering the inbox is fine as long as the board can still discover and act on failures when needed | ❓ Partial |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" and "Progressive disclosure" principles - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers and the expectation of full visibility at all times
### Recommendation The filtering direction is reasonable for a 40-agent org at scale. However, the design should keep failures **visible somewhere** (a separate "Agent Activity" tab is mentioned — that's the right approach), not suppressible entirely. The "option to disable failure notifications in board inbox entirely" is the one ask that conflicts with tree principles. Recommend accepting the filtering/tab-separation direction, and discussing the "disable entirely" ask with maintainers before implementing. A "mark all as read" button is low risk and clearly aligned. --- Reviewed issue: `#3236` · Tree snapshot: `33dc6fd` · Commands: `@gardener re-review` · `@gardener pause` · `@gardener ignore` 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3237.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds GitHub PAT authentication for private skill repos and a "delete by source" endpoint, building on the existing `secretService` infrastructure. The core implementation is solid — previous review concerns (atomic `deleteBySource`, companyId scoping, orphaned secret cleanup, confirmation dialog, and type export) are all resolved. The PR description uses a custom summary format instead of the required template from CONTRIBUTING.md: it is missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Please update the description to follow `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`. Additionally, this PR introduces visible UI changes (PAT field in the import form and `SkillAuthSection` in the skill detail) — before/after screenshots are required per the contribution guide.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to follow the required template and screenshots are added for the UI changes. All previously flagged P0/P1 concerns (atomicity, companyId scoping, orphaned secrets, missing confirmation dialog, type export) are fully resolved. The three remaining inline findings are P2: missing test coverage for two new endpoints, a silent-failure degradation path in PAT resolution, and a UI gap for GHE URLs. None of these block correctness on the primary user path. server/src/services/company-skills.ts (silent PAT resolution failure), ui/src/pages/CompanySkills.tsx (GHE URL PAT field gap), server/src/__tests__/company-skills-routes.test.ts (missing coverage for new endpoints)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/github-fetch.ts | Adds optional `authToken` param to `ghFetch`; sets `Authorization: Bearer` header when provided. Clean, minimal change. | | packages/shared/src/validators/company-skill.ts | Adds `authToken` to import schema and new `companySkillUpdateAuthSchema`; exports `CompanySkillUpdateAuth` type — consistent with existing patterns. | | packages/shared/src/validators/index.ts | Re-exports `companySkillUpdateAuthSchema` and `CompanySkillUpdateAuth` type, resolving the previously flagged missing export. | | packages/shared/src/index.ts | Passes through new validator exports from the validators sub-index; no issues. | | server/src/routes/company-skills.ts | Adds `DELETE /by-source` (correctly registered before `/:skillId`) and `PATCH /:skillId/auth`; auth token is never logged. | | server/src/services/company-skills.ts | Implements PAT storage, `resolveSkillAuthToken`, `updateSkillAuth`, and atomic `deleteBySource`; silent secret-resolution failure can surface as confusing GitHub errors. | | server/src/__tests__/company-skills-routes.test.ts | Tests cover auth token passthrough for import, but `DELETE /by-source` and `PATCH /:skillId/auth` have no test coverage. | | ui/src/api/companySkills.ts | Adds `updateAuth` and `removeBySource` API methods; auth token omitted from request when falsy. | | ui/src/pages/CompanySkills.tsx | Adds PAT import field, `SkillAuthSection`, and delete-by-source confirmation dialog; PAT field not shown for GitHub Enterprise URLs, blocking private GHE repo imports from UI. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/company-skills.ts Line: 1475-1479 Comment: **Silent PAT failure produces confusing GitHub errors** If the secret referenced by `sourceAuthSecretId` has been deleted (e.g. via the Secrets UI), `resolveSecretValue` throws and the catch silently returns `undefined`. All subsequent GitHub calls then proceed unauthenticated, and for private repos the user sees a generic "repo not found" / 401 from GitHub — with no indication that their stored PAT is missing. Consider at minimum logging a warning, or returning a typed sentinel that callsites can surface as a more helpful error message. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/pages/CompanySkills.tsx Line: 1369-1380 Comment: **PAT field hidden for GitHub Enterprise URLs** The regex `/github\.com|^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+\/[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+/` matches `github.com` URLs and bare `owner/repo` strings, but not GHE URLs like `https://ghe.example.com/org/repo` (the scheme prefix prevents the `^owner/repo` anchor from matching, and the hostname contains dots not in the character class). A GHE user with a private repo would see no PAT field during import, causing an authentication failure with no obvious path to retry. The `SkillAuthSection` lets them add a PAT afterwards, but only if the import first succeeds — which it won't for a private repo. Consider broadening the condition to cover any non-`github.com` hostname (e.g. detect that the URL contains a GitHub-style path) or simply always show the PAT field when the source looks like any GitHub-style URL. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/company-skills-routes.test.ts Line: 14-17 Comment: **New routes have no test coverage** `deleteBySource` and `updateSkillAuth` are absent from the mock object and have zero route-level tests. The existing suite verifies auth for `import` and `deleteSkill` but leaves `DELETE /by-source` and `PATCH /:skillId/auth` completely untested — no authorization checks, no happy-path assertions. Consider adding `deleteBySource` and `updateSkillAuth` to `mockCompanySkillService` and covering at least the permission and basic response shape for each new endpoint. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `medium` · commit: `1956ccd` > [!WARNING] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary This PR adds GitHub PAT support for private skill repos and a bulk delete-by-source endpoint. Both features are well-aligned with the adapter abstraction, governance-first, and company-scoped isolation principles. 3239.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces Gemini CLI capability detection (`capabilities.ts`) so that flag support (prompt, model, approval-mode, output-format, resume, sandbox, stdin) is probed at runtime rather than assumed, and extends `runChildProcess` in `server-utils.ts` to pipe prompts via stdin when the CLI supports it. The parse layer and session-retry logic are also hardened, and integration tests cover the new paths. **The PR description is incomplete** — the thinking path contains unfilled placeholders (`[Which subsystem or capability is involved]`, etc.), the model field is `-`, and no checklist items are checked. Please fill these in per CONTRIBUTING.md before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is completed; the code changes are sound with minor design concerns in capabilities.ts. All code-level findings are P2 (design/style), but the incomplete PR description is a clear process requirement per CONTRIBUTING.md. The cachedCaps not being keyed by command is an architectural gap worth addressing before this pattern spreads. packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/capabilities.ts — module-level cache key and stdin detection heuristic.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The `stdin` piping in `runChildProcess` correctly handles EPIPE, and no secrets are logged or exposed through the new capability-probe or parse paths.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/capabilities.ts | New file: probes CLI help text to detect supported flags; module-level cache is not keyed by command, and the stdin fallback heuristic is over-broad. | | packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts | Refactored to use capability-aware arg building and optional stdin prompt delivery; logic looks correct given the capability detection works as expected. | | packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/parse.ts | Extended JSONL parser with broader session ID fields, usage accumulation, and quota/auth detection helpers; changes are thorough and well-structured. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts | Added `stdin` option to `runChildProcess` with proper pipe setup, EPIPE handling, and stream cleanup; implementation looks correct. | | server/src/__tests__/gemini-local-adapter-environment.test.ts | New environment diagnostic tests covering cwd creation, flag probing, and quota classification; tests are clear and well-structured. | | server/src/__tests__/gemini-local-execute.test.ts | New execute integration tests covering prompt delivery, yolo flag, and session resume delta; fake CLI correctly captures both argv and stdin to handle either delivery path. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/capabilities.ts Line: 23-30 Comment: **Module-level capability cache not keyed by command** `cachedCaps` is a module-level singleton that ignores the `command` argument. If two callers in the same process (or the same test file) use different Gemini CLI binaries, the first probe result is returned for all subsequent calls regardless of which binary is requested. In production this matters if agents are configured with different `command` paths; in tests it silently reuses the first probed binary's flags for all later tests within the file. ```suggestion const cachedCapsMap = new Map(); export async function detectGeminiCliCapabilities( command: string, cwd: string, env: Record, ): Promise { const cached = cachedCapsMap.get(command); if (cached) return cached; ``` …and replace `cachedCaps = caps; return caps;` with `cachedCapsMap.set(command, caps); return caps;`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/capabilities.ts Line: 49 Comment: **`supportsStdinPrompt` fallback is over-broad** `!helpText.includes("--prompt")` evaluates to `true` whenever the CLI fails to mention `--prompt` — including when `--help` exits with an error and produces no output at all, making `helpText` the empty string. In that situation, every capability flag would be `false` except `supportsStdinPrompt`, so the prompt would be silently routed to stdin even for a CLI that does not read it, resulting in a hung or no-prompt run. Consider replacing the final clause with an explicit marker and leaving stdin opt-in rather than opt-out: ```suggestion supportsStdinPrompt: helpText.includes("--stdin") || helpText.includes("accepts-stdin"), ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/capabilities.ts Line: 34-35 Comment: **Unsafe `env` cast may hide `undefined` values** The function signature accepts `Record`, but the value is immediately cast to `Record` before being passed to `runChildProcess`. If `env` contains `undefined` values (e.g., from `process.env` spread), Node.js will stringify them as the literal string `"undefined"` in the child's environment. Consider filtering out `undefined` entries before the call: ```suggestion env: Object.fromEntries( Object.entries(env).filter((e): e is [string, string] => e[1] !== undefined), ), ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3240.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR closes a real identity-collision risk in agent authentication by adding a `UNIQUE` constraint to `agent_api_keys.key_hash`, matching the already-correct `board_api_keys` pattern. The migration is well-structured: a PL/pgSQL pre-flight guard raises a clear exception if any duplicate hashes exist before the index is dropped and recreated, keeping the operation safe even on a live database.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the migration is transactional, the pre-flight guard handles the duplicate edge case, and the schema change is minimal and correct. All remaining findings are P2. The only note is a missing Drizzle snapshot, which is an accepted and pre-existing pattern across 8+ hand-written migrations in this repo and does not affect runtime correctness. No files require special attention beyond the noted snapshot omission in the migration directory.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The migration enforces a uniqueness constraint that closes a key-identity collision hole — this is a net security improvement. No secrets, user input, or injectable content is present in the changed files.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/db/src/schema/agent_api_keys.ts | Changed `key_hash` index from `index` to `uniqueIndex`, matching the `board_api_keys` pattern exactly — correct and minimal. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0055_agent_api_key_unique.sql | Adds a PL/pgSQL pre-flight guard for duplicates, then drops and recreates the index as unique — safe and well-structured, but see note on missing snapshot. | | packages/db/src/migrations/meta/_journal.json | Correctly appends the 0055 journal entry with a valid sequential idx and timestamp. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/db/src/migrations/0055_agent_api_key_unique.sql Line: 12-13 Comment: **Missing Drizzle snapshot may cause drift on next `generate`** No `0055_snapshot.json` is included. Because the last snapshot Drizzle sees is `0053_snapshot.json` — where `agent_api_keys_key_hash_idx` is still `"isUnique": false` — the next developer who runs `drizzle-kit generate` will detect a diff between the current `uniqueIndex` schema and the stale snapshot, and will attempt to emit a new migration for the same unique-index change that 0055 already handles. This is consistent with the existing project pattern for hand-written migrations (0004, 0015, 0016, 0022, 0026, 0042, 0043, 0054 all lack snapshots), so the team clearly has a way to manage it — but it's worth confirming the snapshot is intentionally omitted and that future `generate` runs are guarded against producing a duplicate migration. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3244.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR memoizes `CommentCard` and `MarkdownBody` to reduce re-renders on long comment threads. The `MarkdownBody` change is correct and effective. However, the `CommentCard` memoization is defeated at the call site in `TimelineList` because `onVote` is wrapped in a new inline arrow function every render, so `React.memo` will never see stable props for that argument and will always re-render. - **P1 — `CommentCard` memo is a no-op**: `onVote={onVote ? (vote, options) => onVote(comment.id, vote, options) : undefined}` at line 624 creates a new function reference on each `TimelineList` render. The fix is to change `CommentCard`'s `onVote` signature to accept `commentId` as the first parameter and pass the outer reference directly without wrapping.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the unstable `onVote` reference that defeats `CommentCard` memoization. The `MarkdownBody` change works correctly and delivers real perf benefit. The `CommentCard` memo is present but ineffective because the inline arrow function at the call site always creates a new `onVote` reference, so the stated optimization for comment cards won't materialise until that is fixed. ui/src/components/CommentThread.tsx — the `onVote` prop passed to `CommentCard` in `TimelineList`

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/CommentThread.tsx | Wraps `CommentCard` in `React.memo`, but the inline `onVote` arrow function at the call site creates a new reference every render, preventing memo from ever skipping a re-render. | | ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.tsx | Correctly wraps `MarkdownBody` in `memo` and moves `components` into `useMemo` keyed on `theme`, `resolveImageSrc`, and `onImageClick`; will effectively skip markdown re-parsing when props (especially `children`) are unchanged. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `ui/src/components/CommentThread.tsx`, line 624 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/221a3b243d72884f9390bce10f2a93bb84baeb86/ui/src/components/CommentThread.tsx#L624)) **Inline arrow function defeats `CommentCard` memo** A new `(vote, options) => onVote(comment.id, vote, options)` arrow function is created on every render of `TimelineList`. `React.memo` uses shallow (`Object.is`) prop comparison, so `onVote` will always be a different reference — `CommentCard` will re-render on every `TimelineList` render regardless of whether the comment changed. The fix is to stop wrapping `onVote` inline and instead thread the outer stable reference through. Change `CommentCard`'s `onVote` signature to match the parent's (accepting `commentId` as the first argument), and pass it directly: ```tsx // CommentCard prop type — add commentId as first arg to match outer signature onVote?: ( commentId: string, vote: FeedbackVoteValue, options?: { allowSharing?: boolean; reason?: string }, ) => Promise; ``` Then in `TimelineList`, pass the reference directly instead of wrapping it: ```tsx onVote={onVote} ``` And inside `CommentCard`, call `onVote(comment.id, vote, options)` so the component itself supplies the ID.

3244.body: 🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `43394d89` Aligned with context tree. Wraps `CommentCard` and `MarkdownBody` in `React.memo` (with `useMemo` on the markdown `components` object) to skip re-renders during polling cycles. Strictly additive perf fix for long comment threads. Builds on PR #3163's TimelineList memoization. 3246.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new `copilot_local` adapter that routes agent runs through the GitHub Copilot API, resolving credentials via a GitHub PAT exchange, a pre-fetched token, or the `gh` CLI. The overall structure matches existing adapters, but two P1 logic bugs need fixing before merge: - **`execute.ts` line 39**: The `command` default ternary is `isClaudeModel(model) ? \"claude\" : \"claude\"` — both branches are identical, so non-Claude models (gpt-4o, gemini, etc.) will always fall back to the `claude` CLI instead of an OpenAI-compatible one. - **`test.ts` lines 119–123**: The hello probe always injects Anthropic env vars regardless of model, meaning the probe tests the wrong environment for non-Claude model configurations. Additionally, the PR description does not follow the required PR template from `CONTRIBUTING.md` — it is missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used** sections, and the checklist. Please reformat the description using the template at `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not safe to merge — two P1 logic bugs cause incorrect behavior for non-Claude models, and the PR description does not meet the required template format. Two P1 defects: the `command` ternary bug means every non-Claude model silently uses the wrong CLI, and the hello probe injects wrong env vars for non-Claude models, producing misleading test results. Both bugs are on the changed code path. Additionally, the PR is missing all required template sections (Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used, etc.) per CONTRIBUTING.md. `packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/execute.ts` (ternary bug) and `packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/test.ts` (probe env vars and double token exchange).

Vulnerabilities

 - The Copilot token is correctly redacted from invocation logs via `redactKeys` in `execute.ts`. - `GITHUB_TOKEN` and `GITHUB_COPILOT_TOKEN` are also in the redact list. - Token exchange uses `fetch` with a `Bearer` header over HTTPS to `api.github.com` — no plaintext credential exposure. - The `copilot_internal` endpoint is undocumented/internal; if GitHub gates or changes it, token resolution will break silently with a generic HTTP error. - No injection risk identified: the token is injected as an environment variable, not interpolated into shell commands.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/execute.ts | Core adapter execution — has a P1 ternary bug where both branches of the `command` default return `"claude"`, breaking the intended non-Claude CLI fallback. | | packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/test.ts | Environment test — P1: hello probe always sets Anthropic env vars even for non-Claude models; P2: token exchange is called twice per test run. | | packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/token.ts | Token resolution logic with clear priority chain (pre-fetched token → PAT exchange → gh CLI); error handling and status codes look correct. | | packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/index.ts | Adapter metadata and model list; uses an undocumented internal GitHub token endpoint (`copilot_internal/v2/token`) with no stability caveat in comments. | | packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/index.ts | Re-export barrel file; straightforward, no issues. | | server/src/adapters/registry.ts | Registers the new `copilotLocalAdapter` with correct imports and `supportsLocalAgentJwt: true`. | | server/src/adapters/builtin-adapter-types.ts | Adds `"copilot_local"` to the built-in adapter type set; trivial and correct. | | server/package.json | Adds `@paperclipai/adapter-copilot-local` workspace dependency; correct. | | packages/adapters/copilot-local/package.json | New package manifest matching the pattern of other adapter packages; looks correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 39 Comment: **Ternary bug — both branches return `"claude"`** The ternary `isClaudeModel(model) ? "claude" : "claude"` is identical on both sides, so every model (including `gpt-4o`, `gemini-2.0-flash-001`, etc.) will default to the `claude` CLI. The `agentConfigurationDoc` and PR description both say non-Claude models require an OpenAI-compatible CLI, but that fallback default is never actually used. ```suggestion const command = asString(config.command, isClaudeModel(model) ? "claude" : "openai").trim(); ``` (Replace `"openai"` with whatever the expected OpenAI-compatible CLI binary name is for this project.) How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/test.ts Line: 119-123 Comment: **Hello probe always injects Anthropic env vars regardless of model** The probe unconditionally sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, but for non-Claude models (`gpt-4o`, `gemini-2.0-flash-001`, etc.) the adapter sets `OPENAI_BASE_URL` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` instead. This means the probe tests the wrong env configuration for all non-Claude model setups — it will likely fail or produce a misleading "probe passed" result. The probe should mirror the same model-branch logic used in `execute.ts` (lines 135–146): detect `isClaudeModel(model)` and set either the Anthropic or the OpenAI env-var pair accordingly. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/server/test.ts Line: 107-117 Comment: **Double token exchange per `testEnvironment` call** When the token source is `GITHUB_TOKEN` (or `gh auth token`), `resolveCopilotToken` is called twice — once at line 76 for the auth check and again at line 114 for the probe. Each call makes an HTTP request to `api.github.com` to exchange the PAT. The first resolved token (or the error) from step 4 could be reused instead of re-fetching. Consider storing the result from the first call and passing it to the probe section, for example by hoisting the token variable out of the `try` block at line 75 and reusing it below. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/copilot-local/src/index.ts Line: 7 Comment: **Reliance on an internal GitHub API endpoint** `/copilot_internal/v2/token` is an undocumented internal endpoint. GitHub can rename, gate, or remove it without a deprecation notice. If it breaks, the entire adapter silently fails with a token-exchange error. Worth adding a comment in the source (and ideally the docs) acknowledging this is an unofficial API with no stability guarantees. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3248.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR implements tenant isolation for `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` and related wake-context fields (PAY-114) by introducing `sanitizeHeartbeatContextSnapshotForTenant`, which validates `issueId`, `taskId`, `taskKey`, and `issueIds` against the agent's `companyId` before they reach any run, enqueue, coalesce, or process-loss-retry path. All six adapter `execute.ts` files are also updated to defensively delete `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` from the inherited base env when no valid `wakeTaskId` is resolved. The core logic is correct and the call sites look comprehensive. Remaining findings are P2 only: - The `taskKeyCandidate` validation block does not reuse the already-fetched `issueContext` to short-circuit the DB lookup (unlike the `taskId` block), causing a redundant round-trip when `taskKey === issueId`. - The test suite has no case for a standalone cross-tenant `taskKey` value, leaving the `taskKeyCandidate` guard untested.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the security fix is logically sound and applied at all relevant call sites; remaining findings are minor P2 style/coverage suggestions. All findings are P2 (a missing cache short-circuit and a missing test case). The tenant isolation guard itself is correctly implemented and the adapter-layer env-var cleanup is consistent across all six adapters. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts (taskKey cache short-circuit) and server/src/__tests__/pay114-heartbeat-tenant-sanitize.test.ts (missing standalone taskKey test)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds `sanitizeHeartbeatContextSnapshotForTenant` which validates `issueId`, `taskId`, `taskKey`, and `issueIds` against the agent's `companyId` before runs/enqueue/coalesce paths; called at 7 sites. Logic is sound but `taskKeyCandidate` block misses the `issueContext` cache short-circuit used by the `taskId` block. | | server/src/__tests__/pay114-heartbeat-tenant-sanitize.test.ts | New test file covering 4 happy/sad paths for the sanitize function; missing a test for a standalone cross-tenant `taskKey` value (the `taskKeyCandidate` block is untested). | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts | Adds `if (!wakeTaskId) delete env.PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` defensive cleanup to ensure the env var is cleared even when the inherited base env had it set; consistent with other adapters. | | packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/execute.ts | Same `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` env-var defensive deletion pattern added; no issues. | | packages/adapters/cursor-local/src/server/execute.ts | Same `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` env-var defensive deletion pattern added; no issues. | | packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts | Same `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` env-var defensive deletion pattern added; no issues. | | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/execute.ts | Same `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` env-var defensive deletion pattern added; no issues. | | packages/adapters/pi-local/src/server/execute.ts | Same `PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID` env-var defensive deletion pattern added; minor trailing whitespace on line 186 introduced in this PR. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 720-732 Comment: **Missing `issueContext` short-circuit in `taskKey` validation** The `taskId` block (line 690) avoids a redundant DB lookup when the candidate matches `issueContext.id` — the row was already fetched and verified. The `taskKeyCandidate` block always issues a fresh query even when `taskKeyCandidate === issueContext.id`. This causes an extra DB round-trip on every heartbeat wake where `taskKey` and `issueId` are the same valid issue. Applying the same `issueContext &&` short-circuit used for `taskId` would keep the two blocks consistent and avoid the unnecessary query. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/pay114-heartbeat-tenant-sanitize.test.ts Line: 47-54 Comment: **No test for standalone cross-tenant `taskKey`** The `taskKeyCandidate` block in `heartbeat.ts` (lines 720–732) runs when `context.taskKey` holds a value that is neither `HEARTBEAT_TASK_KEY` nor the same as the rejected `issueId` or `taskId`. None of the four tests exercise that path — a cross-tenant `taskKey` that differs from any other field would silently pass the suite even if the guard were removed. Adding a case with only `taskKey` set to a foreign ID (with an empty DB mock response) would close the coverage gap. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3249.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a deduplication guard in the heartbeat post-run flow: before posting a summary comment on an issue, it calls `findRunIssueComment` to check whether the agent already commented during the run, and skips the summary if it did. The logic is correct and reuses the same battle-tested query already in `finalizeIssueCommentPolicy`. The one concern is that the new guard call is placed inside the existing `try/catch`, so a transient DB error from `findRunIssueComment` would be caught, silently suppress the summary post, and log a misleading `\"Failed to post run summary comment\"` message — even though no post was attempted.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge with one low-severity issue to consider: transient DB errors in the guard check will silently suppress summary comments. The behavioral change is narrow and low-risk; the only finding is a P2 error-handling edge case where a flaky findRunIssueComment query swallows the summary comment silently. The happy path works correctly. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — error handling around the new guard query at lines 3350–3356.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds `findRunIssueComment` guard before posting post-run summary; guard is placed inside the existing `try/catch`, which means a transient DB error silently suppresses the summary with a misleading log message. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 3350-3356 Comment: **Guard check swallowed by existing `try/catch`** `findRunIssueComment` is inside the same `try/catch` that was originally only wrapping `addComment`. If the guard query throws a transient DB error, the `catch` block fires, logs `"Failed to post run summary comment"` (a misleading message — nothing was even attempted to be posted), and silently skips the summary. Agents that never post their own comments would lose their summary on a flaky query rather than seeing a retry or the summary still being posted. Consider pulling the guard outside the try so a query failure falls through normally: ```typescript const existingComment = await findRunIssueComment(finalizedRun.id, agent.companyId, issueId); if (!existingComment) { try { const issueComment = buildHeartbeatRunIssueComment(adapterResult.resultJson ?? null); if (issueComment) { await issuesSvc.addComment(issueId, issueComment, { agentId: agent.id, runId: finalizedRun.id }); } } catch (err) { await onLog( "stderr", `[paperclip] Failed to post run summary comment: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}\n`, ); } } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a full memory adapter framework — a `MemoryAdapter` interface, mempalace (ChromaDB-backed MCP) and PARA (file-based) adapters, binding CRUD with target assignment, pre/post-run hooks wired into the heartbeat, a REST audit API, three new DB tables, and supporting UI pages. The architecture is solid: memory failures are non-blocking, company isolation is enforced at both the adapter and DB layers, and the PARA adapter includes proper path-traversal protection.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all remaining findings are P2 style/maintenance concerns; prior P0/P1 issues from earlier review rounds have been addressed. The previous threads' critical issues (upsert stale handle, asyncio.to_thread usage, usage type mismatch) are resolved in the current code. Remaining findings are low-severity: orphaned FK rows that don't affect runtime, fire-and-forget shutdown cleanup, and a markdown formatting quirk in prompt injection. Memory failures are isolated and non-blocking throughout. Minor attention to `packages/db/src/migrations/0053_memory_bindings_and_operations.sql` (missing FK on target_id) and `server/src/app.ts` (signal handler cleanup pattern).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/memory-adapters/mempalace.ts | New mempalace MCP adapter — well-structured with transport-level auto-reconnect; get() is semantically approximate (noted in code comments); upsert fallback synthetic ID partially addressed from prior thread. | | server/src/services/memory-adapters/mempalace-sidecar.ts | New sidecar lifecycle manager — exponential backoff restart, health checking, clean shutdown; logic is correct. | | server/src/services/memory-adapters/para.ts | New file-based PARA adapter — path traversal protection via `toAbsolutePath` with UUID companyId validation; keyword search fallback works correctly. | | server/src/services/memory-hooks.ts | Binding-driven hook system — pre/post-run hooks well isolated with error handling that prevents memory failures from blocking core heartbeat execution. | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Memory context hydration injected before run, capture after run — both wrapped in try/catch so failures are non-blocking; multi-line snippet text rendered as flat bullet items may produce malformed markdown. | | server/src/app.ts | Adapter registration and sidecar lifecycle wired up; SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers call stopMempalace fire-and-forget; remote adapter has no shutdown cleanup. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0053_memory_bindings_and_operations.sql | Additive migration adding 3 tables with proper indexes and FKs; memory_binding_targets.target_id has no FK to agents/companies — orphaned rows possible on agent deletion. | | server/src/routes/memory-bindings.ts | Well-structured REST routes with proper company-scoped authorization; cross-company target validation at creation time is correct. | | server/src/routes/memory-operations.ts | Write/query/forget routes with scope companyId validation; audit log list endpoint with proper pagination and date filtering. | | packages/db/src/schema/memory_operations.ts | Schema correctly types usage column as `MemoryUsageEntry[] | null` — resolves prior thread concern about incorrect type cast. | | server/scripts/backfill-mempalace.ts | CLI backfill tool with checkpoint-based idempotency; duplicates buildCaptureContent from memory-hooks.ts — format drift risk if one is updated without the other. | | packages/shared/src/validators/memory-binding.ts | Comprehensive Zod schemas for all memory API payloads with appropriate length constraints. | | docker/mempalace/serve_http.py | Thin HTTP wrapper around mempalace MCP server; asyncio.to_thread usage documented as intentional for synchronous handle_request; no auth on /mcp endpoint (flagged in prior thread). |

Path: packages/db/src/migrations/0053_memory_bindings_and_operations.sql Line: 1-8 Comment: **`memory_binding_targets.target_id` has no FK constraint** `target_id` is a bare `uuid` with no foreign key to `agents` or `companies`. The route validates agent/company existence at creation time, but the DB won't cascade-delete targets when an agent is deleted. Orphaned target rows will accumulate silently and will continue to be matched by `resolveBindingsForAgent`, making the audit trail misleading. Consider adding a conditional FK (or at minimum a trigger/cleanup job), or at least add a comment in the schema explaining the intentional omission. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. A more reliable pattern defers exit until cleanup completes: ```ts process.on("SIGTERM", () => { stopMempalace().finally(() => process.exit(0)); }); process.on("SIGINT", () => { stopMempalace().finally(() => process.exit(0)); }); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Consider normalizing before injection: ```ts memoryLines.push(`- ${snippet.text.replace(/\n+/g, " ").trim()}`); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. **🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `6896dfb6` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary Re-reviewed after HEAD update (`7f5f43a2` → `6896dfb6`). The architectural concern from the prior review is unchanged: the memory subsystem is still wired directly into `server/src/services/memory-*`, `server/src/routes/memory-*`, and `heartbeat.ts` — it has NOT been moved to the plugin system. The PR has grown and the implementation quality remains high (company-scoped, audited via `memory_operations` table, isolated sidecar failure modes, PARA fallback), but the core-vs-plugin question is still the blocker. ### What's new and what's unchanged **Unchanged between the two HEADs**: architectural placement. `server/src/services/memory-adapters/` (mempalace, PARA, sidecar), `memory-hooks.ts`, `memory-operations.ts`, `memory-bindings.ts`, `routes/memory-bindings.ts`, `routes/memory-operations.ts`, and heartbeat integration all still live in core. **Helpful new concrete comparison point**: [PR #3285 (`feat(plugins): add aws-file-store plugin with MinIO compose`)](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/3285) just landed and demonstrates *exactly* the architectural pattern the tree calls for — it re-scoped a similar "storage-backed agent capability" feature from the CONFLICT version in #3230 into a proper first-party plugin at `packages/plugins/examples/aws-file-store`, using the standard plugin SDK (9 tools, lifecycle hooks, capability manifest, host-owned audit logging). That's the reference implementation for how this kind of subsystem is supposed to integrate. ### Recommendation The two recommendations from the prior review still apply: 1. **Keep in core with explicit rationale** — document a new `product/memory/` or updated `product/agent-model/` tree node that says pre-run hydration and post-run capture are fundamental to the heartbeat protocol, analogous to cost tracking. This is a deliberate scope expansion and needs tree PR first. 2. **Move to a first-party plugin** — use PR #3285 as the template. The `MemoryAdapter` interface in `packages/plugins/sdk/src/types.ts` is already the right abstraction. The gap is that core currently calls adapters directly; instead, a `@paperclipai/plugin-mempalace` (or `plugin-memory-core`) would register the adapter and hook into heartbeat via the plugin event/tool system. The UI pages could live in the plugin's UI slots. The re-review is unchanged: this is a substantial feature that is technically well-built but makes an undocumented architectural decision. Either path is defensible — the decision needs to be made deliberately, not by default. --- Reviewed commit: 6896dfb6 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

## CTO Response — Memory Subsystem Architecture

Thanks for the thorough context review. I've considered both paths and am making a deliberate architectural decision:

**Memory stays in core.** Here's the rationale:

### Core vs. Plugin distinction

The key architectural question is whether memory lifecycle hooks (pre-run hydration, post-run capture) are **orchestration concerns** or **agent capabilities**.

They are orchestration. Specifically:

- **Pre-run hydration** populates agent context before the heartbeat begins — this is system-initiated, automatic, and unconditional for agents with memory bindings. The agent never "calls" it.
- **Post-run capture** extracts learnings after execution completes — again system-initiated, part of the run lifecycle.
- This is analogous to **cost tracking and budget enforcement** — also in core, also system-initiated lifecycle hooks that agents don't opt into.

### Why this differs from aws-file-store (PR #3285)

PR #3285 is the right pattern for **agent-initiated tools** — the agent decides when to read/write files, and the plugin registers tools the agent can call. File storage is an optional capability.

Memory hooks are the inverse: the **system** decides when to hydrate and capture, not the agent. Moving this to a plugin would mean the heartbeat depends on an optional plugin for a non-optional behavior — that's architecturally worse, not better.

### Where the adapter boundary lives

The `MemoryAdapter` interface (`write`, `query`, `get`, `forget`) IS the plugin boundary. Mempalace (ChromaDB-backed MCP) and PARA (file-based) are swappable backends registered through adapter configuration. The core owns the lifecycle; adapters own the storage.

### Documentation path

I'll document this in a `docs/architecture/memory.md` that captures:
- Memory as a core orchestration concern (not an agent capability)
- The adapter interface as the extensibility boundary
- Why the heartbeat lifecycle hooks are non-optional

This is a deliberate scope expansion of core, not an accidental one.

This PR fixes a stale-state bug in the `bundleDraft` initialization effect by replacing an unconditional `if (current) return current` guard with a dirty-check that only preserves the draft when the user has unsaved edits. The fix is logically correct and consistent with the existing `bundleDirty` calculation. **The PR description does not follow the required template from [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).** Specifically, it is missing the required sections: **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** (see `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`). Please fill out the full PR template before requesting merge. Additionally, since this change directly affects whether file content or a placeholder is shown in the UI, **before/after screenshots are required** per CONTRIBUTING.md.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Code fix is correct, but the PR is missing the required template sections and screenshots that CONTRIBUTING.md mandates before merging. The logic change is sound and consistent with the existing bundleDirty computation. The only code-level note is a P2 optimization (spurious re-render on clean refetch). Score is 4 rather than 5 because the PR template (Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) and before/after screenshots are required by CONTRIBUTING.md and are absent. ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx — minor spurious re-render on clean bundle refetch; otherwise correct.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx | Fix is logically correct — replaces unconditional guard with dirty-check so bundle refetches re-sync clean state; minor: returns a new object on every refetch when draft is already clean, causing a spurious re-render. |

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `boardAttentionAt` nullable timestamp that agents can set via PATCH/MCP to escalate an issue to the Board inbox without changing its status or assignee. It renames the inbox "Mine" tab to "Board", adds an "Activity" tab for touched-issue history, hides failed runs from Board by default, and wires the new field into the server query filter and issue detail page. - **P1 — Dismiss does not clear escalation globally**: `archiveIssueMutation` still calls only `issuesApi.archiveFromInbox(id)`; the described `issuesApi.update(id, { boardAttentionAt: null })` pre-call is absent. Escalated issues remain visible to all other board users after one user dismisses them. - **P2 — Missing \"Escalated\"/\"In Review\" filter categories and localStorage persistence**: The PR description states these were added, but neither the `InboxCategoryFilter` type nor the Select component includes them, and no `loadInboxCategoryFilter`/`saveInboxCategoryFilter` functions exist.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to deploy the DB/server/badge changes, but the dismiss-clears-escalation feature described in the PR is unimplemented — escalations persist globally after a board user dismisses an issue. One P1 defect: the described "dismiss clears boardAttentionAt globally" behaviour is completely absent from the code, leaving escalations sticky for all other board users after dismissal. The remaining findings are P2 (missing filter UI, behavioural change to unread tab, no escalation badge test). P1 should be resolved before merge. ui/src/pages/Inbox.tsx — archiveIssueMutation and InboxCategoryFilter; ui/src/lib/inbox.ts — badge test coverage for boardAttentionAt

correctly gates clearing to board users only. | | server/src/services/issues.ts | Added `boardQueueUserId` filter using OR of `assigneeUserId`, `status=in_review`, and `boardAttentionAt IS NOT NULL`. | | ui/src/lib/inbox.ts | Added `activity` tab, hide-failures preference helpers, and changed badge count to include escalated issues; missing `loadInboxCategoryFilter`/`saveInboxCategoryFilter` described in PR. | | ui/src/pages/Inbox.tsx | Renamed "Mine"→"Board", added "Activity" tab, moved board queue query; archiveIssueMutation does not clear boardAttentionAt on dismiss despite PR description; 3252.body:
I have updated the approach and PR description
Please review it again

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `medium` · commit: `b3f660b` > [!NOTE] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary This PR adds a `boardAttentionAt` escalation primitive that lets agents signal "I need a human" without side-effecting issue status or assignee, and cleans up Board inbox noise by adding focused filter categories (Escalated, In Review, Assigned to me), hiding terminal-status issues from the Board default view, and moving the "Hide agent failures" toggle into an Options popover. The changes are well-aligned with Paperclip's governance-first and surface-problems-don't-hide-them principles, and the progressive-disclosure approach (amber banner in issue detail + filter categories in inbox) fits the thin-core/rich-edges pattern. **Note on Greptile's P1/P2 findings:** After reading the diff directly, the Greptile P1 ("dismiss does not clear escalation globally") and P2 ("missing filter categories / localStorage persistence") are **inaccurate for this version of the PR**. The `archiveIssueMutation.mutationFn` correctly calls `issuesApi.update(id, { boardAttentionAt: null })` before archiving when the issue is escalated. The `escalations`, `in_review_issues`, and `assigned_to_me` filter categories exist in `InboxCategoryFilter` (moved to `ui/src/lib/inbox.ts`), and `saveInboxCategoryFilter` is called on every filter change. The escalation badge test is also present in `inbox.test.ts`. Greptile appears to have evaluated an earlier version or missed the type definition move.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Governance / Board control surface | Add a channel for agents to signal "I need a human" without side-effecting task status | Governance node: "The Board is not just an approval gate — it is a live control surface. The human can intervene at any level at any time." Board powers always available. | ✅ Aligned | | Auditable everything | `boardAttentionAt` is a field on the issue, mutated via the same PATCH endpoint; clears globally when board user dismisses | Governance node: "Every mutation writes to `activity_log`. The system is designed so that nothing happens silently." | ✅ Aligned | | Board-only clear guard | Non-board actors return 403 when attempting to set `boardAttentionAt: null` | Governance node: "Governance rules are control-plane invariants enforced at the server, not at the adapter or agent level." | ✅ Aligned | | Tasks as communication channel | Agents escalate via `boardAttentionAt` on an existing issue rather than through a side-channel | Task system node: "All agent communication flows through the task system. There is no separate messaging or chat system." | ✅ Aligned | | Surface problems, don't hide them | Amber escalation banner in issue detail, red dot badge for escalations, terminal-status Board cleanup | Tree principle: "Surface problems, don't hide them" | ✅ Aligned | | Progressive disclosure | Escalation summary in inbox badge → filter categories → amber banner in issue detail with Clear action | Tree principle: "Progressive disclosure (summary → checklist → raw logs)" | ✅ Aligned | | Company-scoped isolation | No cross-company data; `boardQueueUserId` filter is scoped via `companyId` from the existing route guard | Tree decision: "All entities scoped to `company_id`. Never leak data across companies." | ✅ Aligned | | Dismiss side-effect risk | If `issuesApi.update` succeeds but `archiveFromInbox` fails, escalation is already cleared — intentional per PR | PR documents this as "Acceptable — the issue stays visible and the user can re-dismiss." This is a reasonable UX tradeoff; not a governance concern. | ✅ Aligned | | Clear button visible to all actors on IssueDetail | Board-only guard is server-side; agents viewing their own issue detail could see a "Clear" button that returns 403 on click | Governance: "Unified API, Different Authorization." The server guard is correct, but the UI shows a control that silently fails for non-board users — a minor UX inconsistency worth noting. | ❓ Partial |

### Recommendation Ship with one minor follow-up to consider: the "Clear" button in `IssueDetail.tsx` is rendered for all viewers of an escalated issue, but it silently fails (403) for non-board actors. Since agents can view their own issues, they'll see a button they can't use. A lightweight client-side guard (hide the button unless the current session is a board user) would be consistent with Paperclip's "surface problems, don't hide them" principle — surface the escalation state, but don't show a control that isn't available. This is low-severity and can be done as a follow-up. --- Reviewed commit: b3f660b · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3254.body:
## Additional finding

The error also appears in a variant with backticks around the keywords:

```
Claude run failed: subtype=success: API Error: 400 messages.1.content.0: Invalid `signature` in `thinking` block
```

This means the regex fix needs to be more flexible to handle both formats:

```ts
// Instead of exact match:
/invalid signature in thinking block/i

// Use a flexible pattern that handles backtick-wrapped variants:
/invalid[\s\`']*signature[\s\S]*?thinking/i
```

3257.body:
**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · issue updated: `2026-04-10T02:08:04Z` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary The feature request is valid and the underlying need is real — board users can't reliably receive out-of-band alerts when agents need intervention. However, there is a direct tension between two tree decisions that requires a deliberate maintainer call: the governance-first principle argues this belongs in core, while the "thin core, rich edges" principle says notification delivery (email/webhook) belongs in plugins.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Board governance reachability | Board users must receive alerts when agents need intervention, even if the UI is closed | Board has unrestricted oversight; "surface problems, don't hide them"; budget hard-stops notify the board | ❓ Partial | | Core vs. plugin boundary | Requesting email/webhook notification as core infrastructure | "Thin core, rich edges" — knowledge bases, external integrations, and special surfaces go into plugins, not core | ⚠️ Conflict | | Triggering events | `assigneeUserId` change, `blocked` status, approval created, budget threshold | Budget hard-stops already emit high-priority activity events; board notification on budget threshold is already in the governance model | ❓ Partial | | Plugin as workaround | Reporter built a `board-notify` plugin using Resend | Plugin SDK supports `ctx.events.on()` subscriptions and `ctx.http.fetch()` for outbound calls — the plugin pattern works | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Thin core, rich edges": external integrations go into plugins; governance-first - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board hard-stops and budget threshold events already defined; board oversight is non-negotiable - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin event subscriptions and outbound HTTP via `ctx.http.fetch`; plugin extensibility is the designed escape hatch
### Recommendation The maintainers need to decide: is notification delivery infrastructure (email/webhook routing) a governance primitive that belongs in core, or an integration concern that belongs in a first-party plugin? Both positions are defensible: - **Core argument:** Board reachability is a safety property, not a feature. If agents can't reliably page the human, governance fails silently — which directly contradicts "surface problems, don't hide them." - **Plugin argument:** Email providers (Resend, SES, SMTP), webhook formats, and filtering logic vary per operator. The "thin core, rich edges" principle exists precisely to avoid baking in opinionated integrations that not every operator wants. A middle path worth considering: add a lightweight **notification hook abstraction** in core (event → configurable webhook POST) with no email logic, and ship first-party email plugins separately. This preserves the thin core while making the default experience work out of the box. The reporter's `board-notify` plugin demonstrates the plugin path is viable today. If the plugin SDK has the right event hooks and capability grants, promoting a reference plugin to "bundled by default" may be the right answer rather than adding email logic to the server. --- Reviewed issue updated: 2026-04-10T02:08:04Z · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a bug where the inbox badge counted all `mineIssues` (up to 100) rather than only those with `isUnreadForMe: true`, causing "Mark all as read" to leave a stale non-zero count. The fix is a one-line filter in `computeInboxBadgeData` backed by a new targeted test. The PR description does not follow the required PR template from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`. It is missing the **Thinking Path**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections — all of which are required by CONTRIBUTING.md for every PR. Since this is a UI/badge change, before/after screenshots are also expected per the template and CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Code change is safe to merge — minimal, correct, and well-tested; only the PR description needs to be updated to match the required template. The one-line fix is correct and directly addresses the stated bug. The new test explicitly covers the read/unread filtering case, and all 21 existing tests continue to pass. No logic, data-integrity, or security concerns were found in the changed code. The only gap is the missing PR template sections (Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used, Checklist, screenshots), which are process requirements and do not affect code correctness. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/lib/inbox.ts | Added `.filter((issue) => issue.isUnreadForMe)` to `visibleMineIssues` in `computeInboxBadgeData` — targeted, correct fix for the badge overcounting bug | | ui/src/lib/inbox.test.ts | Added "excludes read issues from the badge mineIssues count" test that directly validates the fix with a mixed read/unread set | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix: inbox badge counts all issues inste..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/ba179a6e370d23c13c6bd4652024b61da9ce6652) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=27949521)

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a comprehensive Company Files knowledge base feature: a `knowledge_entries` table, 10 REST endpoints, a three-tab UI at `/:companyPrefix/files`, and manifest injection into the agent heartbeat so all six adapters receive relevant file/document context on demand. The PR description **does not follow the required PR template** from `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` — it is missing the mandatory **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Risks**, and **Checklist** sections. Since this is a visual change (new sidebar nav item, new page), **before/after screenshots** are also required. Please fill out the full template before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the empty-name validation bug and filling out the required PR template sections. One P1 logic bug (empty name accepted on PATCH rename) needs to be fixed before merge. The PR template is also missing required sections (Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks, screenshots). The remaining findings are P2 quality suggestions. server/src/routes/knowledge.ts (empty-name validation on PATCH) and server/src/services/heartbeat.ts (unbounded manifest injection).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/knowledge.ts | New 10-endpoint router for the knowledge feature; missing `name` validation on the PATCH rename route allows empty/whitespace names to be persisted. | | server/src/services/knowledge.ts | Core CRUD service with recursive CTE-based department resolution and BFS delete cascade; unsafe `tx as unknown as Db` cast and no storage cleanup on delete are minor concerns. | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Injects knowledge manifest into agent context on every heartbeat; no size cap means large knowledge bases will bloat every agent prompt. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0055_knowledge_entries.sql | New `knowledge_entries` table with correct FK references, composite indexes, and partial unique indexes on document/asset IDs. | | ui/src/pages/CompanyFiles.tsx | New 874-line page with three-tab layout, folder tree, markdown editor with 1.5 s autosave, and file upload dialog; logic is clean with no obvious bugs. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts | Adds `renderPaperclipKnowledgePrompt` utility that builds a markdown manifest from the knowledge entries array; safe with array/null guards. |

Comments Outside Diff (2)

 1. `server/src/services/knowledge.ts`, line 976-978 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/66c2574fb8b15cfbec9bb8dfeaf8ed134c8537a6/server/src/services/knowledge.ts#L976-L978)) **Unsafe transaction cast may silently break** `tx as unknown as Db` casts a Drizzle transaction object to the full `Db` type. Currently only `execute` is called on it, which transactions do support, but if `Db` gains new methods (or if the call site is refactored), this will silently pass type-checking while failing at runtime. Passing `tx` through a narrower interface (or overloading `getDescendantIds` to accept either) would be safer. Longer-term, consider extracting a minimal `DbLike` interface (`{ execute: … }`) that both `Db` and transaction satisfy.

Passing `tx` through a narrower interface (or overloading `getDescendantIds` to accept either) would be safer. Longer-term, consider extracting a minimal `DbLike` interface (`{ execute: … }`) that both `Db` and transaction satisfy. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
2. `server/src/services/knowledge.ts`, line 1003-1008 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/66c2574fb8b15cfbec9bb8dfeaf8ed134c8537a6/server/src/services/knowledge.ts#L1003-L1008)) **Storage objects orphaned on delete** Asset DB rows are removed inside the transaction, but the underlying storage objects (S3/GCS blobs etc.) are left behind permanently. 3262.body:
**🌱 gardener:verdict:** \`CONFLICT\` · severity: \`high\` · commit: \`a4690b3\` > [!WARNING] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary This PR adds a knowledge base as a first-class core feature (DB table, 10 REST endpoints, heartbeat injection across all 6 adapters, UI page), which directly contradicts three explicit tree decisions: knowledge bases belong in plugins not the core control plane, Paperclip is explicitly "not a knowledge base", and knowledge base was deferred from V1 scope.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Knowledge base in core | Adds `knowledge_entries` table, service, and 10 REST routes directly into server core | "Thin core, rich edges. Knowledge bases… go into plugins — not the core control plane." (`product/NODE.md`) | ⚠️ Conflict | | Paperclip identity | Positions knowledge base as a first-class Paperclip feature | "Paperclip is explicitly **not**: …a knowledge base…" (`product/NODE.md`) | ⚠️ Conflict | | V1 scope | Ships as a new core feature | "Explicitly deferred from V1: …knowledge base…" (`product/NODE.md`) | ⚠️ Conflict | | Adapter integration | Injects knowledge manifest into all 6 adapter heartbeat prompts | Adapter abstraction is aligned; the question is whether the data source should be core vs plugin | ❓ Partial | | Company-scoped isolation | All queries are scoped to `company_id`; auth follows existing `assertCompanyAccess` pattern | Company-scoped isolation (`engineering/NODE.md`) | ✅ Aligned | | Audit trail | All mutations call `logActivity` with before/after details | Governance-first; every mutation logged (`product/NODE.md`) | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Product strategy, anti-requirements, V1 scope; explicitly lists knowledge base as out-of-scope for core and for V1 - [`engineering/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/NODE.md) — Backend architecture; service-oriented pattern; company-scoped isolation
### Recommendation This work is solid in implementation quality — the scoping, audit trail, and adapter integration are all well-executed. The conflict is with *where* it lives, not *whether* it should exist. Two paths forward: 1. **Move to a plugin** (tree-aligned): Extract the knowledge base into a plugin using the Plugin SDK. The heartbeat injection via `renderPaperclipKnowledgePrompt` and the knowledge service logic can live in a plugin that registers routes, injects into heartbeat context, and owns the DB migration. The tree's "thin core, rich edges" decision exists precisely to protect the control plane from scope creep. 2. **Update the tree first** (if the team has decided knowledge base is now core): If product has deliberately changed direction on this, update `product/NODE.md` to remove the anti-requirement and un-defer knowledge base from V1 scope — then land this PR. That PR should be discussed and merged before this code lands. Either way, this PR should not merge until the conflict is resolved. The tree is the decision record; the code should follow from it. --- Reviewed commit: a4690b3 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

## Response to gardener context review

We investigated the plugin path thoroughly. After an exhaustive audit of the Plugin SDK (41 capabilities, 13 UI slot types, all data persistence APIs), the plugin architecture **cannot support a company file system** without significant degradation:

| Requirement | Core | Plugin SDK | Impact |
|-------------|------|-----------|--------|
| Folder hierarchy with referential integrity | ✅ Self-referencing `parentId` FK | ❌ Flat JSONB with parentId field | No cascade delete, no tree integrity |
| Relational data model (FKs to documents, assets, agents) | ✅ `knowledge_entries` table | ❌ `plugin_entities` JSONB blobs only | No referential integrity, no indexes |
| Document revision tracking | ✅ Reuses `documents` + `document_revisions` | ❌ Manual versioning in JSONB | No audit trail on document changes |
| File upload + storage | ✅ multer + StorageService | ❌ "Future scope" per plugin spec | No binary files at all |
| Agent context awareness | ✅ Lightweight manifest in heartbeat | ❌ Agent must explicitly call tool | Agents don't know files exist unless they search |
| Department scoping via org tree | ✅ Recursive CTE on `agents.reportsTo` | ❌ No SQL access | Cannot efficiently resolve subtrees |
| Custom REST API (10 endpoints) | ✅ Express routes | ❌ Only webhook + bridge proxy | No direct API |

### The case for core

The gardener classifies this as "a knowledge base." It isn't. It's a **company file system** — structured folders and documents scoped to company, department, and agent.

Every real organization has files. Not a knowledge base product, not a wiki, not Notion — just a structured place where persistent company files live. This is as fundamental as having an org chart or a budget. A company without a file system is incomplete infrastructure.

- **Org charts** are core → they define who reports to whom
- **Budgets** are core → they define spending limits
- **Approval policies** are core → they define governance gates
- **Company files** should be core → they define everything else the company needs to persist

The plugin system can't provide this because it was designed for external integrations (Linear sync, GitHub webhooks) — not organizational primitives. The gaps (no schema, no hierarchy, no uploads, no revision tracking) prove the point.

### Tree PR

Per the gardener's guidance, we've opened a PR to update the context tree:

**[serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree#1](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/pull/1)** — proposes distinguishing "general-purpose knowledge base / wiki" (still deferred) from "company file system" (foundational infrastructure).

Happy to hear the team's perspective.

Extracts adapter eligibility for the instructions-bundle UI into a shared helper (`supportsInstructionsBundles`) and adds `gemini_local` to the supported set, fixing the missing Instructions tab for Gemini agents. The change is low-risk and the logic is correct. Since this makes the Instructions tab visible for Gemini agents (a UI change), please add before/after screenshots per the contribution guidelines.

Set matches `adapter-display-registry.ts` local entries exactly. | | ui/src/lib/instructions-bundles.test.ts | Regression test for `gemini_local` and two negative cases; pre-existing adapters in the set are not covered, so accidental removal of e.g. `claude_local` would go undetected. | | ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx | Replaces inline 6-way OR check with `supportsInstructionsBundles`; semantically equivalent plus the new `gemini_local` case. No logic changes outside the gate. |

Path: ui/src/lib/instructions-bundles.test.ts Line: 4-13 Comment: **Tests only cover the new adapter and two negatives** The suite verifies `gemini_local → true` and two non-local adapters, but none of the six pre-existing entries (`claude_local`, `codex_local`, `opencode_local`, `pi_local`, `hermes_local`, `cursor`) are asserted. Accidentally deleting any of those from the set would silently break those agents' instructions-bundle visibility without a failing test. 3265.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a route-doubling bug on company switch: `toCompanyRelativePath()` previously stripped the company prefix only when the second path segment was in `BOARD_ROUTE_ROOTS`. Plugin page routes (e.g. `youtube-trends`) don't appear in that set, so the prefix was preserved in localStorage and then re-prepended during navigation. The fix changes the logic to strip the prefix whenever the first segment is **not** a known global or board route root — a more robust allowlist-to-denylist flip. Regression tests are included for plugin routes, board routes, and global routes. The PR description does not follow the required PR template (`PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`). The following sections are missing: **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Risks**, and the **Checklist**. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, all sections are required before merge — please fill them in.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to follow the required template — no code-level blockers found. The logic change in `toCompanyRelativePath` is correct and well-tested; all remaining findings are P2 (missing PR template sections), which do not affect runtime behavior. No files require special attention on the code side; the only gap is the incomplete PR description.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/lib/company-routes.ts | Core fix: `toCompanyRelativePath` now strips the company prefix for any path whose first segment is not a known global or board route root — correctly handling plugin page routes that were previously missed. | | ui/src/lib/company-routes.test.ts | Adds regression tests for plugin page routes, board routes, and global routes against `toCompanyRelativePath`; coverage is appropriate for the bug fixed. | | ui/src/lib/inbox.test.ts | No changes to inbox logic visible; test file appears to have been included in the PR's broader merge commit but contains no issues. | | ui/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx | Dashboard page changes (company kill-switch UI, plugin slot outlet) appear unrelated to the route-fix but look correct on review. | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["Fix plugin page routes doubling company ..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/0c4871637040231613f98f5b5ddb7d06951b3eca) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=27952338)
