For gardener, `@gardener re-review` as a comment does the same thing without changing the HEAD SHA. --- Reviewed commit: b6c6c76b · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

@greptile-apps review

P2 findings addressed in commit 3893420c — prune off-by-one moved to SELECT WHERE clause with regression test, `svc.remove(id, agentId)` scoped to close the TOCTOU window. Description updated to match the PR template. Please re-review.

Thanks @serenakeyitan — I went through each of the 3 concerns. TL;DR: the PR template is now filled (it was updated right after your bot's review snapshot at `3893420c`), and the coordination/placement concerns aren't actually what they look like.

### 1. PR template

Updated to the full CONTRIBUTING.md template (Thinking Path + What Changed + Why + Verification + Risks + Model Used + Checklist + Greptile follow-up) via browser edit at ~11:10Z — your bot's review was at ~10:30Z so that section is stale. Should be clean on `@gardener re-review`.

### 2. Core vs plugin placement

I looked at what actually lives in each layer before answering:

- **Existing agent-scoped state tables in core**: `agent_task_sessions`, `agent_runtime_state`, `agent_api_keys`, `agent_config_revisions`, `agent_instructions`. All small fixed-schema agent-lifecycle state, all in `packages/db/src/schema/` + `server/src/services/`. Not plugins.
- **What plugins actually do**: see `packages/plugins/examples/*` — external integrations (file storage, notifications, etc.), each with its own manifest, no ability to declare new core DB tables or mount routes under `/agents/:id/…`.
- **What this PR creates**: a small fixed-schema agent-scoped primitive with `pg_trgm` indexes, `assertAgentIdentity` gating, and a 500 rows × 4 KB/agent hard cap. No retrieval augmentation, no embeddings, no external services.

Putting this in plugins would require the plugin SDK to own schemas + indexes + route-layer auth guards + FK cascades to `agents` and `companies`. That's a much bigger change than what plugins currently do.

If you see this as "a knowledge base", I'd push back on that framing — it's literally a per-agent sticky-notes table with keyword search, no smarter than `issue_comments` (which is also in core).

### 3. Coordination with #3250

I diffed the file lists and there's **zero overlap at the schema/route/service layer**:

| | **#3289 (this PR)** | **#3250 (mempalace)** |
|---|---|---|
| Tables | `agent_memories` | `memory_bindings`, `memory_binding_targets`, `memory_operations` |
| Routes | `/agents/:id/memories` | `/memory-bindings`, `/memory-operations` |
| Service dir | `server/src/services/agent-memories.ts` | `server/src/services/memory-adapters/{mempalace,para}.ts` + `memory-hooks.ts` |
| Plugin SDK | no touch | adds `MemoryAdapter`, `MemoryScope`, hydration/capture types |
| Search | `pg_trgm` keyword | semantic via ChromaDB / file |
| Driver | **agent** (explicit `save`/`search` calls) | **framework** (pre-run hydration, post-run capture, prompt injection) |
| LLM calls | none | embeddings for vector search |
| Size | +1,183 lines | +7,361 lines, 43 files |

These are different primitives at different layers, not two implementations of the same thing. #3289 is "agent-controlled explicit notes"; #3250 is "framework-controlled retrieval-augmented context". A well-designed system likely wants both — an agent that wants to explicitly remember "user prefers French" should not need to wait for a semantic-search adapter pipeline.

Per your own table, this PR is ✅ aligned on 4 of 6 axes (auth, isolation, audit, resource bounding) — the only ❓ is the core/plugin call and the only ⚠️ is the "two memory subsystems" framing which I think is a misread of #3250's scope. Happy to coordinate with @davison if the maintainers want a formal decision recorded in the tree, but I don't think either PR should be blocked on the other.

cc @davison — do you see any actual overlap I'm missing?

hey 👋🏻 

Not sure about the overlap, haven't looked in great detail at what this is providing but there seems to be some slight similarity of intent. I think the obvious point that the gardner plugin just missed completely is that the one I created is more about token cost than fact retrieval. The injection from a mempalace store is an attempt to add better context to the current run, it's not targeted fact retrieval. Feels more complementary than competitive.

With mempalace and my PR (which is still evolving), the interaction is transparent and injects relevant compressed content into the agent's heartbeat or run, and then transparently saves the entire transcript back at the end. The next part of it, which I think will be the more valuable part, is that this gets extended to agent instructions, paperclip API documents and other resources that the agent is presented with at the start of a run and which costs so many tokens to process every time.

But mine comes with a cost (the bit of gardeners analysis that I agree with) in having to setup and manage a sidecar to get this benefit. Their should be choices about what paperclip users want to adopt for their particular case. This plugin and PR looks good.

Are you on the paperclip discord? I'm going to post to the #dev channel there shortly about it. I'll cc you on it (let me have your username if not the same as your github. On discord I'm `autistic shark`.

Thanks @davison — and yes, that framing is exactly right. Your read is the one I'd hoped a human would land on: **token-cost-driven automatic context injection** (mempalace) and **agent-driven explicit fact recall** (this PR) are two halves of the same problem, not competitors. Gardener saw "memory" twice and pattern-matched to overlap without reading what each primitive actually does.

If anything they compose cleanly. An agent that learns "user prefers French" today has no way to pin it durably — mempalace's compressed context injection is optimizing for recency and relevance across a big corpus, so that single short fact can easily get summarized away or fall off the window. `paperclipMemorySave` gives the agent an explicit "don't forget this" primitive it can call when it KNOWS something matters. The two don't need to know about each other — mempalace can read from `agent_memories` as just another content source if it wants to, or not, and it still works.

The direction you mentioned (extending context injection to agent instructions + Paperclip API docs + other run-start resources) is the genuinely high-value next step — those are the biggest per-run fixed costs and the ones that hurt most at scale. Looking forward to seeing where mempalace goes there.

I'm not on the Paperclip Discord yet, so feel free to just @ me on GitHub (or reply here) if you want me in on any follow-up discussion — happy to coordinate on whether it makes sense for `agent_memories` to eventually implement a `MemoryAdapter` interface from your plugin SDK work, so the explicit-save primitive can plug into the same composition surface the sidecar adapters use.
Withdrawing this PR — I'm redirecting my efforts to my own fork. Thanks for maintaining paperclip.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `b853ad7a`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Excellent fix. The author correctly identified that `listAdapterModels(type)` and `detectAdapterModel(type)` ignore `companyId` entirely — the `assertCompanyAccess` gate was guarding nothing and silently breaking the onboarding dropdown. Replacing it with `assertBoard` matches the tree's "Unified API, Different Authorization" pattern: the auth check reflects what data is actually exposed, not what the URL looks like.

What makes this PR stand out is the careful scoping discipline: `POST /test-environment` is explicitly left strict because it resolves per-company secrets via `secretService.normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` / `resolveAdapterConfigForRuntime` — loosening that one would be a real cross-company leak. Drawing that exact line, and explaining it in both the PR description and an inline test comment, is the right level of care for an auth change.

Test coverage is thoughtful too — the 95-line test file explicitly notes the `local_implicit` god-mode bypass and constructs realistic remote session-board actors to exercise the cross-company path. This closes a subtle test gap where default `createApp()` would have hidden the fix.

Also worth noting: this is yet another instance of the "silent failure" pattern I've been flagging in recent items (#3282, #3215, #3207, #3284). The 403 → empty dropdown with no clear error is the exact anti-pattern the tree's "surface problems, don't hide them" principle calls out. Worth a general audit: any endpoint gated by `assertCompanyAccess` that doesn't actually read `companyId` in its handler is likely misscoped for the same reason.

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `663720c0` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary The fix unblocks a real and painful onboarding failure (opencode binary segfaults under Rosetta). But softening a validation error into a warning log sits in direct tension with two load-bearing tree decisions: **"surface problems, don't hide them"** and **"no silent auto-recovery"**. The trade-off deserves an explicit maintainer call before landing.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Unblock flaky probe | Let agent creation succeed when probe throws | "Surface problems, don't hide them" (product/NODE.md) | ❓ Partial | | Demote error to warn | `throw unprocessable(...)` → `logger.warn(...)` | "No silent auto-recovery" — the tree explicitly favors bubbling failures to the actor | ❓ Partial | | Lazy re-validation path | `/adapters/opencode_local/models` catches the same config at first run | Task-system assumes issues surface where the work happens — acceptable fallback | ✅ Aligned | | Keep instrumentation | `logger.warn` still logs stack + adapterType + companyId | Auditability — operators can still trace | ✅ Aligned | | Probe can't distinguish flaky vs. real misconfig | Both paths get demoted | The server can't tell them apart — this means real misconfigs also get deferred | ⚠️ Conflict |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" (decision #6) - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "No automatic recovery, no automatic reassignment" - [`adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) — adapter lifecycle and validation contract
### Questions for maintainers 1. **Can the user see the warning?** A `logger.warn` lives in server logs that most end users never look at. If an agent is created with a genuinely broken opencode config, the user sees "success" at create-time and then a failure at first run. That's the scenario the tree explicitly calls out as the anti-pattern. Consider: return `200` with a structured `warnings: [{code, message}]` field in the response body so the UI can show an inline "probe failed, here's why" banner — keeps onboarding unblocked AND surfaces the problem. 2. **Is there a narrower fix?** Two alternatives that address the root cause instead of the symptom: - **Detect Rosetta and skip the probe**: check for the `x86_64` arch running under `arm64e` kernel (macOS) and short-circuit the probe with a specific "probe skipped on Rosetta" log line. Users on normal arm64 or amd64 still get the pre-flight validation. - **Retry the probe**: if the flakiness is intermittent (the PR says "intermittently"), 2-3 retries with small backoff would fix flakiness without demoting real failures. 3. **Does the UI show the warning?** Worth confirming the onboarding flow can render a structured warning alongside "agent created". If not, this fix makes the bug invisible to end users even when the probe surfaces a genuine misconfig. ### Recommendation Don't block on unblocking the Rosetta use case — it's a real pain. But before landing this exact fix, consider: 1. Picking a root-cause path (Rosetta detection, retry, or opt-in probe) that preserves create-time validation for honest failures. 2. If that's not feasible in this PR's scope, then at least return the warning in the API response body so the UI can surface it — demoting to server logs alone violates the "surface problems" principle in practice, even if the error is technically still observable. 3. Either way, add a test case for a genuinely invalid opencode config (not just a flaky probe) to prove the lazy-validation path catches it at first run. --- Reviewed commit: 663720c0 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). That's a boundary question: should `skills/` be strictly for Paperclip-meta skills (things that teach agents how to operate inside Paperclip) and general productivity skills should live elsewhere (separate repo, community skills marketplace, plugin)? The PR ships 615 + 411 lines of Python (`build_report.py`, `validate_html.py`) with no visible test files. The validator is a security boundary — worth adding tests that exercise the negative examples in `examples/bad-*.json` to prove the validator rejects them. ### Recommendation Don't block on the design — the skill is well-scoped and the security posture (inline CSS only, no JS, content whitelist, separate validator script) is the right shape. But before landing: - Clarify the "default" claim in the PR title/description and the maintenance plan for skill registration. - Get a maintainer call on whether `skills/` is a control-plane-only directory or a general first-party library, and document that decision in the tree. - Add Python tests for the validator that cover the `bad-external-css.json` and `bad-script.json` negative examples. --- Reviewed commit: da225e31 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3294.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes three things: the OpenClaw gateway schema (Paperclip context is now sent via `extraSystemPrompt` instead of a rejected root `paperclip` key), the Claude CLI hello probe (exit 1 with valid stream-json on rate limits is classified as a `warn` rather than a hard error), and two smaller fixes (UI gateway token field, embedded Postgres `createPostgresUser` for uid=0). - The `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` fallback regex `/limit|quota|rate/i` is applied whenever `is_error: true`, which is broad enough to misclassify legitimate configuration errors (context length exceeded, invalid rate settings, etc.) as rate-limit warnings — hiding real probe failures and reporting a `\"warn\"` status instead of `\"fail\"`. - The PR description is missing required sections from the PR template (`CONTRIBUTING.md`): **Thinking Path**, **Risks**, and **Model Used** (\"Made with Cursor\" does not satisfy the required format). Additionally, the gateway token field is a visible UI change that requires before/after screenshots per the contribution guidelines.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Mostly safe to merge after addressing the overly-broad rate-limit regex and filling out the required PR template sections. One P1 finding: the broad `/limit|quota|rate/i` fallback in `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` can silently downgrade real probe errors to warnings, making environment diagnostics unreliable. All other changes (schema fix, default URL, root Postgres, UI token field, process-defaults) look correct and well-tested. packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts — the final rate-limit regex path needs tightening.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts | Adds `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput`; the final regex path is too broad and can misclassify non-rate-limit errors as rate-limit warnings during the probe. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts | New module: resolves dangerously-skip-permissions and builds the root-headless allowed-tools list; logic is sound. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/test.ts | Probe flow updated to classify exit-1 + rate-limit stream-json as warn; uses the broad `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` which may hide real failures. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Paperclip context moved from root `paperclip` key to `extraSystemPrompt` block; schema-compliance fix looks correct. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts | Adds `openclawGatewayToken` handling; correctly writes token to `adapterConfig.headers["x-openclaw-token"]` during create flow. | | server/src/index.ts | Adds `createPostgresUser: true` when running as root for embedded Postgres; straightforward uid=0 fix. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts Line: 192-193 Comment: **Broad regex can misclassify real errors as rate-limit warnings** The final fallback — `is_error: true` + `/limit|quota|rate/i` — is too permissive. Any error whose `result` text happens to contain "limit", "rate", or "quota" for non-rate-limit reasons (e.g. `"Context length limit exceeded"`, `"Processing rate invalid"`, `"Evaluation quota not configured"`) will be reported as `claude_hello_probe_rate_limited` (warn) instead of `claude_hello_probe_failed` (error), silently hiding real configuration failures during the probe. Consider tightening to patterns that actually describe usage/billing limits: ```suggestion return /rate[\s_-]?limit|usage[\s_-]?limit|quota[\s_-]?exceed|you'?ve\s+hit\s+your\s+(usage\s+)?limit|resets\s+\d|billing[\s_-]?limit/i.test(resultText); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts Line: 22-48 Comment: **`Monitor` tool absent from root headless allowlist** The `CLAUDE_ROOT_HEADLESS_ALLOWED_TOOLS` list covers most built-in Paperclip tools but omits `Monitor`. Agents running as root that rely on `Monitor` for streaming background-process events will fail at runtime without an explicit `--allowed-tools` override in `extraArgs`. If `Monitor` is intentionally excluded (e.g. it is unsupported in headless mode), a comment explaining the omission would help future maintainers. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3294.body:
**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `5ba2663a` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary The individual fixes are all technically reasonable and several align well with the tree. Two product-fit concerns deserve a maintainer call: (1) the PR bundles four logically distinct fixes, which makes review and bisect harder; (2) several of the changes implicitly expand "running Paperclip as root" into a supported deployment mode, which contradicts what `infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md` documents.
Context match per sub-fix | Sub-fix | Tree guidance | Fit | |---------|---------------|-----| | OpenClaw context → `extraSystemPrompt` | Adapter owns agent-param shaping | ✅ Aligned | | OpenClaw token via env var / `_FILE` | Secrets are provider-abstracted, `*_FILE` matches Docker/K8s pattern | ✅ Aligned | | Claude rate-limit reclassification | "Surface problems, don't hide them" — classifies but doesn't hide | ✅ Aligned | | Claude root-UID auto-disable `--dangerously-skip-permissions` | Adapter knows its CLI constraints | ✅ Aligned | | `CLAUDE_ROOT_HEADLESS_ALLOWED_TOOLS` hardcoded allowlist including `Bash(*)` | Governance-first / least privilege | ❓ Partial | | Embedded PG `createPostgresUser: true` when uid=0 | `infrastructure/deployment` documents UID/GID remapping, not running as root | ❓ Conflict | | Implicit expansion of "run as root" deployment mode | "UID/GID remapping for volume permissions" pattern already exists | ❓ Partial | | UI gateway token field in `CreateConfigValues` | Secrets should flow through `secretService.normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` | ❓ Unclear |
Tree nodes referenced - [`infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — "UID/GID remapping for volume permissions" — the docker entrypoint remaps the `node` user and `gosu`s to it, which means Paperclip should not run as uid 0 in production - [`adapters/claude-local/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) — Claude adapter owns CLI invocation and flag resolution - [`adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md) — OpenClaw gateway transport and context shaping - [`engineering/backend/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Secrets are provider-abstracted
### Questions for maintainers 1. **Should this be split into two or three PRs?** The four sub-fixes are: - OpenClaw schema + token resolution (adapter concern) - Claude rate-limit reclassification (adapter concern) - Root-UID handling across claude-local + embedded-PG (deployment concern) - UI gateway token field (frontend concern) Landing as one bundle makes the "run as root" story hard to discuss independently from the OpenClaw fix. Splitting lets each get a focused review and makes bisect/revert cleaner. 2. **Is "run Paperclip as root" now a supported mode?** The `infrastructure/deployment` tree node documents that the Docker entrypoint uses `gosu` to drop to the `node` user specifically to avoid running as root. This PR adds code paths that only fire when Paperclip runs as root: - `createPostgresUser: true` for embedded-postgres - Auto-disable `--dangerously-skip-permissions` for Claude CLI - Inject `--allowed-tools` default allowlist These fixes only make sense if "run as root" is a deliberately supported mode. If so, the deployment tree node should be updated to describe the two modes (gosu-remapped vs. root-direct) and when each is appropriate. If not, the fixes may be protecting a misconfiguration that operators should be correcting upstream (e.g., by setting `USER_UID`/`USER_GID` correctly). 3. **Is the `CLAUDE_ROOT_HEADLESS_ALLOWED_TOOLS` list tight enough?** The comment in the code frames this as "safer than --dangerously-skip-permissions", but `Bash(*)` is effectively unrestricted shell access, and the list also includes `Write`, `Edit`, `WebFetch`, `Task`, `RemoteTrigger`, `EnterWorktree`. That's the same blast radius as `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, just expressed differently. Two alternatives worth considering: - Keep the hardcoded default but add a logger.warn at startup explaining "Paperclip is running as root; Claude is configured with a broad tool allowlist. Run as a non-root user for finer-grained permission control." - Make the allowlist configurable per deployment via env var or adapter config so operators can tighten it without patching the code. 4. **UI gateway token field** — confirm that `CreateConfigValues.openclawGatewayToken` flows through `secretService.normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` and is stored in the vault, not in plain JSON in `agent.adapterConfig`. The PR description mentions adding the field but not the secret-handling path. ### Recommendation Don't block on the core direction — the individual fixes address real bugs. But before merge: - **Split the PR** into an OpenClaw/Claude adapter fixes PR and a separate "run as root" deployment mode PR. The former can land quickly; the latter deserves a deliberate maintainer call and tree documentation. - If the maintainer call is that "run as root" is supported, **update `infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md`** to describe both modes and their trade-offs. - **Confirm the secrets path** for the UI gateway token field. - **Add a startup warning** when running as root with the broad default allowlist, so operators are explicitly aware of what they're getting. The claude-local rate-limit reclassification is a clean improvement and would land fine on its own. The OpenClaw schema fix is also a clean adapter-level bugfix. Everything else needs a bit more deliberation. --- Reviewed commit: 5ba2663a · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

Superseded by the split requested in review:

- **#3296** — OpenClaw gateway adapter fixes + Claude hello probe / rate-limit detection + heartbeat test (no root mode, no UI token).
- **#3297** — Root/systemd `claude_local` defaults, OpenClaw create-token → `adapterConfig.headers`, startup warning, deployment doc section, UI persistence hint.

Closing this PR in favor of those two avoids duplicate merge risk.

Greptile Summary

This PR extracts a centralized `adapterConfigDefaults` dictionary and `getCreateValuesForAdapterType()` utility to replace three separate inline default maps across `NewAgent.tsx`, `OnboardingWizard.tsx`, and `AgentConfigForm.tsx`, ensuring `dangerouslySkipPermissions` (and other adapter-specific fields) are consistently seeded regardless of which UI flow creates or edits an agent. The `asBoolean` server utility is also extended to coerce string `"true"`/`"false"` values, and a thorough regression test suite is added covering both the new defaults module and the full server-side propagation chain.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all findings are P2 style/consistency improvements with no runtime impact on the current codebase. The core refactor is correct and well-tested. The three P2 comments (opencode_local borrowing Claude's constant, leftover direct imports in OnboardingWizard, and dangerouslySkipPermissions not included in the edit-mode adapter-switch overlay) are all currently harmless since every adapter shares the same `true` default today. No P0 or P1 issues were found. ui/src/components/agent-config-defaults.ts (cross-adapter constant coupling), ui/src/components/OnboardingWizard.tsx (leftover direct imports)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/agent-config-defaults.ts | New shared module — logic is correct; minor concern that `opencode_local` borrows `DEFAULT_CLAUDE_LOCAL_SKIP_PERMISSIONS` from the claude-local package, creating cross-adapter coupling. | | ui/src/components/agent-config-defaults.test.ts | 14 tests covering dictionary entries and `getCreateValuesForAdapterType()`; thorough and well-structured. | | ui/src/components/OnboardingWizard.tsx | `buildAdapterConfig()` correctly uses `adapterConfigDefaults`, but the adapter-switching click handlers still import and use the individual adapter constants directly — leftover duplication the PR intended to remove. | | ui/src/components/AgentConfigForm.tsx | Create mode correctly delegates to `getCreateValuesForAdapterType()`; edit mode adapter-switch overlay resets `dangerouslyBypassSandbox` from defaults but omits `dangerouslySkipPermissions`, creating a minor inconsistency. | | ui/src/pages/NewAgent.tsx | Local `createValuesForAdapterType()` cleanly replaced with the shared `getCreateValuesForAdapterType()`; no issues. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts | `asBoolean` extended to coerce string `"true"`/`"false"` values — safe and useful for DB round-trips. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.test.ts | New `asBoolean` tests cover all edge cases including null, numbers, non-boolean strings, and case-insensitive coercion. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/index.ts | Exports `DEFAULT_CLAUDE_LOCAL_SKIP_PERMISSIONS = true` constant for use by shared defaults. | | server/src/__tests__/dangerously-skip-permissions.test.ts | Comprehensive regression tests for end-to-end `dangerouslySkipPermissions` propagation through all server layers; `noopSecretsSvc` mock correctly deduplicated. | | server/src/routes/agents.ts | No functional changes; untouched by the PR's core refactor. |

Those handlers could instead use `adapterConfigDefaults[nextType]?.model ?? ""`, eliminating these three imports and keeping `adapterConfigDefaults` as the sole source of truth for per-adapter model defaults.

<!-- gardener:state · reviewed=fdecac8e2e40686059bf5e90131f992bebc38c33 · verdict=ALIGNED · severity=low · tree_sha=33dc6fd046c372a3bbb88c0de72121c4c8f3df50 -->
<!-- gardener:last_consumed_rereview=none -->

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `fdecac8e`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns that block merge, but a few worth noting for reviewers.

The core change is a clean single-source-of-truth refactor: three UI codepaths (`NewAgent`, `OnboardingWizard`, `AgentConfigForm`) each carried their own inline per-adapter default dictionaries, which had drifted. Extracting `adapterConfigDefaults` + `getCreateValuesForAdapterType()` eliminates the drift. Server-side normalization in `applyAdapterConfigDefaults` is actually MORE correct than UI-side defaults alone — API callers (CLI, scripts, plugins) now get the default too, not just users creating via the web UI. Good instinct.

### Notes for reviewers

1. **PR scope is broader than the description suggests.** The PR body says "UI-only default logic" and "no server, or adapter runtime changes" but the diff includes:
    - Server-side normalization in `server/src/routes/agents.ts` (`applyAdapterConfigDefaults` now writes a `dangerouslySkipPermissions` default for `claude_local` and `opencode_local`)
    - A behavior change to `asBoolean` in `packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts` (now accepts string `"true"`/`"false"` case-insensitively, was boolean-only)
    - A new 314-line `dangerously-skip-permissions.test.ts`

    Both extra changes are safe and well-tested, but the "low risk, UI-only" framing is misleading. Worth updating the PR description so CODEOWNERS/reviewers see the full scope.

2. **Interaction with PR #3294 (still open).** PR #3294 introduces `resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions()` in the claude-local adapter that overrides the configured value to `false` when Paperclip runs as root. If both PRs land, the data flow becomes:
    - UI writes `dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` from the shared default
    - Server normalizes to `true` if missing (this PR)
    - Adapter overrides to `false` at execute time on root (#3294)

    The config stored in the DB says one thing; the runtime does another. That's not a bug per se, but it creates a surprising diagnostic experience — operators will see `dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` in agent config but no `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag in the transcript. Worth coordinating with #3294 or adding a logger.info explaining the override when it fires.

3. **`asBoolean` widening is a cross-cutting behavior change.** The new string coercion (`"true"` → `true`, `"false"` → `false`, case-insensitive) affects every caller of `asBoolean` across the codebase. The widening is backwards-compatible (existing boolean inputs behave identically), and the coercion only fires on string inputs that previously fell through to the fallback. The test coverage is thorough. Low risk, but worth calling out because adapter-utils contract changes are the kind of thing that can surprise plugin authors or downstream adapters — the utility's signature lives in `@paperclipai/adapter-utils` which is part of the stable surface.

4. **`dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` as the default** is a load-bearing security decision. The inline comment explains it correctly (headless mode has no interactive prompts), but it's worth documenting in the context tree under `adapters/` as an explicit policy so future adapter authors know the rationale and don't re-litigate it.

### Recommendation

Land it. The refactor is good, the server-side enforcement is the right layer, and the tests are solid. Just (a) update the PR description to reflect the full scope, (b) add a coordination note to PR #3294 about the config-vs-runtime override interaction, and (c) consider adding a one-paragraph note to `adapters/NODE.md` or the tree about the "headless mode default skip" policy so the rationale is discoverable.

Re-reviewed after HEAD update (`4977b80e` → `89dcb8a2`). The new commit is "detect rate limits via per-line stream-json, not stdout regex" — meaningful improvement. Moving rate-limit detection from a stdout regex to per-line JSON parsing is more robust: handles multi-line output correctly, doesn't false-positive on content that happens to contain "limit" or "quota" in other contexts, and integrates cleanly with the existing stream-json parser. Scope and tree alignment unchanged from prior cycles.

Greptile Summary

This PR makes two targeted fixes: (1) the OpenClaw gateway adapter now moves Paperclip context out of a root `paperclip` key (rejected by OpenClaw's `additionalProperties: false` schema) into `extraSystemPrompt`, and falls back to `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` when no URL is configured; (2) the claude-local hello probe now classifies a Claude CLI exit-1 with valid rate-limit stream-JSON as a warning rather than a hard failure, with a new exported `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` helper and matching tests. The PR description does not follow the required PR template from `CONTRIBUTING.md`. Please add the missing sections before merging: - **Thinking Path** — trace from Paperclip's purpose down to this specific change - **What Changed** — formal bullet list - **Verification** — how to confirm it works (the test plan is a good start but should be in this section) - **Risks** — behavioral shifts worth calling out (e.g. gateway URL-missing no longer fails fast) - **Model Used** — provider, exact model ID/version, and capability details (not just "Made with Cursor") - **Checklist**

Confidence Score: 4/5

Code changes are correct and well-tested, but the required PR template sections are absent and should be filled in before merge. All three inline findings are P2 (false-positive risk in raw stdout scan, flaky exit-code assertion in the loopback test, and unused resolveClaimedApiKeyPath). However, the missing PR template (Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) is a stated merge blocker per CONTRIBUTING.md, keeping the score at 4 until that is addressed. packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts (raw stdout scan), packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts (flaky exitCode assertion), packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts (unused helper).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts | Adds `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` — logic is sound but the raw stdout scan can false-positive on assistant text containing limit-related phrases. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/test.ts | Hello probe updated to treat exit 1 + valid stream-JSON as a success path when rate-limited; logic and fallback detail handling are correct. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Moves Paperclip context from root `paperclip` key to `extraSystemPrompt`; adds default loopback URL; `resolveClaimedApiKeyPath` is defined but unused (hardcoded path in `buildWakeText`). | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts | URL-missing failure changed to URL-defaulted info; new test asserts `exitCode === 1` which will fail if OpenClaw is running on the loopback port in CI. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/defaults.ts | New file exporting `DEFAULT_OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_WS_URL = "ws://127.0.0.1:18789"` — clean and correct. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts | Config builder now explicitly sets default URL, timeoutSec, and waitTimeoutMs; straightforward and correct. | | server/src/__tests__/openclaw-gateway-adapter.test.ts | Test assertions updated to match new `extraSystemPrompt` payload shape and URL-defaulting behaviour; changes are aligned with the implementation. | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-comment-wake-batching.test.ts | Heartbeat batching expectations updated to check `extraSystemPrompt` instead of `paperclip` root object; correct alignment with schema change. | | server/src/__tests__/claude-local-parse-rate-limit.test.ts | New unit tests for `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` covering the key detection paths; coverage is adequate for the three main cases. | | server/src/__tests__/claude-local-adapter-environment.test.ts | Integration test using a fake Claude CLI that emits rate-limit stream-JSON and exits 1; correctly validates the new probe classification path. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/index.ts | Re-exports `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` from `parse.ts`; no issues. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/index.ts | Minor export additions consistent with the rest of the adapter's public surface. |

Comments Outside Diff (2)

 1. `packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts`, line 281-290 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/f9a89328eca0eccbe0d83e7dfed18ba0b3504b0d/packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts#L281-L290)) **Test assumes nothing is listening on loopback port 18789** The renamed test ("uses default loopback gateway URL when url is omitted") passes `{}` as config, which now resolves to `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`. Greptile Summary

This PR adds two deployment-related features on top of #3296: (1) root-process safety for `claude_local` — when `uid=0`, `--dangerously-skip-permissions` is suppressed and a default `--allowed-tools` list is injected so headless runs still work; and (2) an optional gateway token field for the OpenClaw adapter create flow, written to `adapterConfig.headers['x-openclaw-token']`. The PR description does not follow the mandatory PR template from `CONTRIBUTING.md`. It is missing: a **Thinking Path** (blockquote format), a **What Changed** section, a **Verification** section, a **Risks** section, a **Model Used** section (only \"Made with Cursor\" is provided — the template requires provider, exact model ID/version, and capability details), and the **Checklist**. Additionally, `config-fields.tsx` introduces a visible UI change (new token field) but no before/after screenshots are provided, which are required by CONTRIBUTING.md for UI changes.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Code changes are functionally sound but the PR description is missing all required template sections and no UI screenshots are provided — both are hard requirements per CONTRIBUTING.md before merge. All remaining P2 findings are style/quality suggestions. The score is held at 4 solely because the PR does not satisfy the mandatory PR template (missing Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) and omits screenshots for a UI change — these are explicit merge-blockers per CONTRIBUTING.md, not code correctness issues. No files need code-level attention; the blocker is the PR description and missing UI screenshots.

Security Review

 - **Plain-text token storage** (`ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx`, `packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts`): `x-openclaw-token` is written directly into `adapterConfig.headers` as plain JSON in the agent record. It is not passed through `normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` and therefore bypasses the secrets vault. The value is visible in any API response or DB backup that returns the agent config. The PR documents this trade-off explicitly, but operators should be aware. - **Broad `--allowed-tools` list injected for root runs** (`packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts`): When running as `uid=0`, Paperclip injects a pre-approved tool list (`Bash(*)`, `Write`, `WebFetch`, etc.) so headless Claude runs can proceed. This is a deliberately broad surface by design; operators are warned at startup and can tighten it via `extraArgs`.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts | New module: cleanly encapsulates root-uid detection, `dangerouslySkipPermissions` resolution, and `--allowed-tools` injection logic; well-tested via imports in execute and test paths. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts | Adds optional `openclawGatewayToken` → `adapterConfig.headers['x-openclaw-token']`; token written as plain-text JSON into agent record (acknowledged in PR, no vault normalization). | | ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx | Adds `SecretField` for gateway token on both create and edit paths; correctly migrates `x-openclaw-auth` → `x-openclaw-token`; UI change with no screenshot provided. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts | Integrates `process-defaults` functions correctly; `--allowed-tools` injection and `--dangerously-skip-permissions` skipping on root are wired up in both `buildClaudeArgs` and the probe path. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/test.ts | Probe path correctly uses `resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions` and `shouldInjectRootHeadlessAllowedTools`, aligned with execute.ts behavior. | | server/src/__tests__/claude-local-parse-rate-limit.test.ts | New unit tests for `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` covering rate-limit event detection, error result detection, and normal success — good coverage. | | server/src/__tests__/claude-local-adapter-environment.test.ts | Adds integration test verifying exit-1 + rate-limit stream JSON is classified as `claude_hello_probe_rate_limited` warn, not a probe error — good regression coverage. | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-comment-wake-batching.test.ts | Removes assertion on `secondPayload.paperclip.wake` structure, replacing with `toBeUndefined()` + `extraSystemPrompt` checks — appears intentional protocol change from #3296. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts | Adds optional `openclawGatewayToken` to `CreateConfigValues`; backward-compatible as optional field. | | server/src/index.ts | Adds root-uid startup `logger.warn` summarizing Postgres and Claude allowlist behavior; warning is clear and actionable. | | docs/deploy/deployment-modes.md | New 'Process user: root vs non-root' section documents the trade-offs clearly with a comparison table. | | ui/src/components/agent-config-defaults.ts | Adds `openclawGatewayToken: ""` to `defaultCreateValues` — correct default, keeps the field optional without breaking existing create flows. |

Path: ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx Line: 138-145 Comment: **Gateway token stored as plain-text JSON** The hint correctly tells operators that `x-openclaw-token` is persisted as plain JSON in the agent record and not passed through `normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` into the secrets vault. This means the token is readable via any API endpoint or DB query that returns `adapterConfig`, and would appear in database backups in the clear. The PR acknowledges this trade-off, which is appreciated. Consider adding a one-line note in the on-edit `SecretField` (line 199) as well, since operators editing an existing agent face the same storage behaviour and currently see no hint there. <!-- gardener:state · reviewed=b3c1082123f19ececdad66bb579208a3731aca08 · verdict=ALIGNED · severity=low · tree_sha=33dc6fd046c372a3bbb88c0de72121c4c8f3df50 -->
<!-- gardener:last_consumed_rereview=none -->

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `b3c10821`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Clean adapter feature. Mirrors the existing Claude login recovery flow exactly, which is the right precedent — Paperclip control plane exposes a UI action, the adapter owns the CLI invocation using its managed `CODEX_HOME` strategy, and the `codex_auth_required` error code lets the UI react when auth is missing mid-run. No cross-adapter abstraction leaks, no governance concerns, and existing API-key support is untouched.

Tree alignment is straightforward:
- **Adapter abstraction**: authentication lifecycle lives in the adapter, not in core orchestration
- **Surface problems, don't hide them**: the new `codex_auth_required` error code is a structured signal, not a silent failure
- **Unified API**: the agent-detail UI action is a parallel of the existing Claude login button — one UI surface, adapter-specific wiring behind it

One small note: the PR description mentions `pnpm build` still fails on Windows because the repo build script uses `cp -r`. That's unrelated to this PR and worth a separate issue so it doesn't block Windows contributors.

Minimal, well-scoped fix. Claude adapter's instruction wrapper now explicitly treats the `instructionsFileDir` as the authoritative base for sibling files like `./HEARTBEAT.md`, `./SOUL.md`, `./TOOLS.md` — closes an onboarding-time failure where fresh CEO agents tried to read siblings from the parent agent directory instead of the managed bundle directory. This is exactly the kind of adapter-owned path semantics the tree expects to live inside the adapter package, and the regression test asserts the stronger directive during the resume-to-fresh fallback path. No cross-domain impact, no bundle persistence change. Related to prior issue #3191 which flagged a similar "sibling file resolution" class of bug in the managed AGENTS.md bundle — worth the reviewer confirming both paths now resolve consistently.

3300.body: **🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `high` · commit: `4ce3604e` > [!WARNING] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. This review flags a scoping issue that should be addressed before any part of this PR lands. ### Summary The stated fix is legit — re-reading `index.html` per request instead of caching at startup closes a real black-page bug on SPA direct navigation after a UI rebuild. But **this PR is not a small fix**. It is a 74-commit, 147-file, 12,834-addition bundle that spans months of fork-internal work: adapter failure taxonomy, heartbeat/memory gates, Telegram integration, self-hosted OpenTelemetry stack, remote_trigger adapter, agent bundles (`cowork/agents/*`), new plugin examples, workflows, and more. The stated fix is ~0.1% of the actual diff. This is almost certainly a mistaken branch target. The branch name is `fix/anga-766-spa-routing-stale-index-html` but it contains commits from dozens of unrelated ANGA-* tickets. Either the author meant to open a branch with only the ANGA-766 commits, or the fork branch was never rebased onto a clean base for the upstream PR. ### Concerns 1. **Impossible to review as one PR.** 74 commits across adapter, heartbeat, plugins, observability, telegram, agent bundles, and workflows is far beyond what any reviewer can sanely evaluate in one sitting. Even if every change is individually correct, they should be reviewed independently. 2. **Potential fork-internal content leak.** The diff includes `cowork/agents/{brand-artist,career-monitor,cto,dev-agent-platform,dev-agent-products,learning-agent-2,operations-lead,visibility-agent,youtube-ingest}/` instruction bundles, Taskfile additions, `doc/AGENT-GIT-WORKFLOW.md`, `doc/PUSH_CONVENTION.md`, and a plan doc at `docs/plans/2026-04-01-claude-rate-limit-fallback-investigation.md`. Some of that looks like org-internal material (agent personas, internal workflow docs) that the author may not intend to open-source. Worth double-checking before any of this lands. 3. **Possibly conflicting changes with in-flight PRs.** This branch touches several files that are currently under review in other PRs: - `packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts` and `parse.ts` — also touched by PRs #3296, #3297, #3299 - `packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts` — also touched by #3296, #3297 - `packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/execute.ts` — also touched by #3298 - `packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts` — conflicts with ongoing gemini work Landing this as-is would create hard-to-resolve merge conflicts for multiple open PRs. 4. **Scope laundering risk.** A PR whose title claims a 9-line fix but contains 12k lines of unrelated changes defeats the "small, reviewable PRs" model and the governance principle that every change should be independently auditable. This is the exact inverse of the scoping discipline we just saw on PR #3294 → PR #3296/#3297 (which split one bundled fix into two focused PRs). ### Recommendation **Before any part of this lands**: 1. **Close this PR and open a small, focused one for the ANGA-766 fix** (re-read `index.html` per request + the matching test). That fix is legitimate and should land on its own — estimated size: 1 file, ~10 lines, plus a regression test. 2. **If the author wants to contribute the other changes upstream**, open separate PRs per logical unit: - Adapter failure taxonomy + fallback chain (ANGA-184/205) — one PR - Self-hosted OpenTelemetry observability stack — one PR (large, but architecturally distinct) - `remote_trigger` adapter type — one PR - Memory pre-flight gate (ANGA-165) — one PR, and coordinate with PR #3250 / #3289 (two active memory PRs) - Telegram integration — one PR (note: this may conflict with "Thin core, rich edges" tree guidance — telegram is likely plugin territory) 3. **Audit the diff for fork-internal content before re-opening.** Agent bundles under `cowork/agents/` and internal workflow docs may not be intended for upstream. ### Scope laundering note for gardener runbook Gardener should probably flag any PR whose diff size is dramatically larger than what the title suggests, regardless of alignment, because scope laundering is itself a governance concern. This PR is the clearest example I've seen — stated as a 9-line SPA routing fix, delivered as 12,834 lines across 74 commits. --- Reviewed commit: 4ce3604e · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

Addressed review feedback: I force-pushed a cleaned branch so this PR now contains only the ANGA-766 SPA navigation fix in server/src/app.ts. Scope creep has been removed; PR is now reviewable as a focused bugfix.

3301.body:
**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `medium` · issue snapshot: `2026-04-10T12:58Z` > [!NOTE] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary Clear, well-diagnosed bug. Aligns with an existing class of "tool errors treated as terminal failures" bugs in the opencode-local adapter, and the tree's "surface problems, don't hide them" principle gives a clean framing for the fix. **This is the same root cause as #3193** — worth linking so a single fix addresses both symptoms.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Tool-error vs run-error classification | Keep guardrail/tool errors in the agent's thought chain, not bubble as terminal failure | "Surface problems, don't hide them" — silent termination on recoverable errors is the anti-pattern | ✅ Aligned | | Adapter-owned error shaping | Fix belongs in opencode-local JSONL parser / lifecycle code | Adapters parse agent output into the shared `TranscriptEntry` types | ✅ Aligned | | Control plane boundary | Paperclip surfaces exit code; adapter decides what's terminal | "Adapters do NOT own task scheduling" but DO own "parsing agent output" | ✅ Aligned | | Self-correction loop | Agent learns from adapter error and retries within the same run | Autonomous tasks must be able to recover from recoverable errors | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) — adapter lifecycle and output parsing contract - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter abstraction, three-layer package structure, session management - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — task communication and recovery semantics
### Duplicate root cause Issue #3193 ("opencode_local treats tool_use errors as terminal adapter failures") reports the same class of bug from a different angle: tool-level errors in the OpenCode JSONL stream are being folded into the same terminal-error signal as run-level failures. This issue (#3301) is the user-facing symptom of that same misclassification, specifically for the "Read before Write" guardrail. A single fix to the JSONL parser in `packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/parse.ts` (or equivalent) should address both. Worth linking the two issues and treating them as one work item. ### Recommendation — solution ranking The reporter proposes three solutions. Ranking them by tree alignment and blast radius: 1. **Fix the JSONL parser first** (implied by #3193 but not yet done). This is the root-cause fix: tool errors stay in the transcript, only fatal adapter errors bubble up as run-level failures. Scoped to the adapter package, no cross-domain changes. Do this regardless of the other options. 2. **OpenCode server API transition** (reporter's option 1) is architecturally attractive but much larger scope — it would enable persistent sessions and finer-grained error recovery, but deserves a dedicated design discussion and tree node under `adapters/opencode-local/` describing the lifecycle shift. Not a prerequisite for fixing this bug. 3. **Retry-loop logic in Paperclip** (reporter's option 2) should be avoided — it crosses the adapter boundary. Paperclip's control plane shouldn't know about "adapter_failed" strings or special-case opencode error patterns; that's the adapter's job to classify correctly. 4. **`--session`/`--continue` flags** (reporter's option 3) is a useful supplement to option 1, but only after the parser is fixed. If the parser still classifies tool errors as terminal, CLI-level session persistence doesn't help. ### Related tree observations This is now the fifth or sixth instance of the "something silently fails instead of being recoverable" pattern I've flagged across recent PRs and issues in this gardener run (#3282, #3215, #3207, #3284, #3290, and now #3301/#3193). Worth considering whether `adapters/NODE.md` should grow a short section on error classification conventions — specifically "tool errors vs adapter errors vs run errors" — so future adapter authors have a clear rule to follow. The current split looks like each adapter invents its own boundary. --- Reviewed snapshot: issue@2026-04-10T12:58 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

It would be best to link to the opencode server rather than use the CLI, as that is where the development is headed.
I presume it would also reduce server resources, as each agent would hook into the existing server rather than create its own.

However, this was causing me real pain.

## Temporary solution
opencode already has a built-in bypass: set `OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true` before starting the CLI.
Example:
`OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true opencode`
Or export it in your shell:
`export OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true`

What it does:
- Disables the "read before overwrite" check
- Also disables the "file changed since last read" protection

Where it lives:
- packages/opencode/src/file/time.ts:90
- packages/opencode/src/flag/flag.ts:73

## How To in Paperclip
In each agent, you can set OpenCode Environment Variables.

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Direct fix for issue #3264 by the same reporter (`@harperaa`) who filed it — nice closing of the loop. The change matches the proposed fix exactly: instead of enumerating board routes and stripping only those, the new logic strips the company prefix whenever the first segment is not a global or board route. That's the right inversion — it treats plugin page routes as first-class citizens of the routing layer without requiring every new plugin to register itself in a central enumeration, which aligns with the tree's "thin core, rich edges" stance toward plugins.

The regression tests are well-structured: three separate test cases covering plugin page routes (the bug), board routes (to prove the fix doesn't regress existing behavior), and global routes (to prove the fix doesn't over-strip). The docstring explicitly links back to issue #3264, which makes the test self-documenting for future readers.

Two-line code change, 24-line test file. Perfect scope. Once this lands, issue #3264 can be closed.

3532.body:
**Pocholo-agents routing decision — 2026-04-13**

Valid intake for asertu Agents. Routing to Devi-agents for a server feature.

**Scope (per the issue's own suggested fix):**
- In the issue PATCH route (`server/src/routes/issues.ts` or equivalent), after a status transition to `in_review`, enqueue a wakeup for `createdByAgentId` if set and different from the current assignee, using `wakeReason: "execution_review_requested"`
- Additionally, if the issue has a `parentId`, query the parent issue's current `assigneeAgentId` and enqueue a wakeup for them as well

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. When agent A delegates an issue to agent B and B sets it to `in_review`, agent A receives a heartbeat wakeup with `wakeReason: "execution_review_requested"`
2. When a child issue moves to `in_review`, the parent issue's current assignee is also woken
3. No double-wakeup when the creator and current assignee are the same agent
4. No regression in the existing `backlog → active` and `assigneeChanged` wakeup paths

**Priority:** Medium — multi-agent delegation chains stall silently at handoff without this.
**Routing:** Devi-agents, server-only change.

## Workaround (self-hosted, while waiting for upstream fix)

Until wakeup-on-in_review is implemented server-side, you can work around this by **combining the status change with an explicit reassign** in a single PATCH. The assignee change already triggers `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup`, so the reviewer is woken immediately:

\`\`\`bash
BODY=$(printf '{"status":"in_review","assigneeAgentId":"%s"}' "$REVIEWER_UUID")
curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $PAPERCLIP_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-Paperclip-Run-Id: $PAPERCLIP_RUN_ID" \
  "$PAPERCLIP_API_URL/api/issues/$PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID" \
  --data-raw "$BODY"
\`\`\`

We updated all agent `HEARTBEAT.md` instruction files to enforce this pattern, with explicit reviewer UUIDs per role (Backend/Frontend → QA, QA → CTO, CTO/CMO/UX → CEO). This eliminates the silent stall without any server change.

3540.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR replaces four hardcoded adapter-type lists in server routes and UI components with declarative capability flags (`supportsInstructionsBundle`, `instructionsPathKey`, `requiresMaterializedRuntimeSkills`) on `ServerAdapterModule`, exposed through the adapter listing API. All built-in adapters in `registry.ts` were updated to declare flags explicitly, and the UI now fetches capabilities from the API via a new `useAdapterCapabilities` hook with synchronous fallbacks for the first render.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all built-in adapter behavior is preserved through backwards-compatible fallbacks and the new flag declarations are consistent with the old hardcoded lists. No P0/P1 findings. The capability-flag logic is correct, the `resolveInstructionsPathKey` default fallback (line 100) properly handles `supportsInstructionsBundle: true` without `instructionsPathKey`, and the react-query caching prevents redundant API calls. The two P2 comments are cosmetic consistency issues with no runtime impact. server/src/adapters/process/index.ts and server/src/adapters/http/index.ts — only built-in adapters missing explicit capability flag declarations.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/adapters/registry.ts | All inline built-in adapters updated with explicit capability flags; `process` and `http` adapters (imported from separate files) were not updated and remain without explicit flags. | | server/src/routes/agents.ts | Adds `adapterSupportsInstructionsBundle` and `resolveInstructionsPathKey` helpers with correct fallback logic; `supportsInstructionsBundle: true` without `instructionsPathKey` correctly defaults to `"instructionsFilePath"` at line 100. | | server/src/routes/adapters.ts | Adds `buildAdapterCapabilities` helper and exposes `capabilities` object on each adapter in `GET /api/adapters`; `supportsSkills` is correctly derived from `listSkills`/`syncSkills` presence. | | ui/src/adapters/use-adapter-capabilities.ts | New hook fetches adapter capabilities from the API and falls back to `KNOWN_DEFAULTS` for built-in adapters on first render; external adapters not in `KNOWN_DEFAULTS` get `ALL_FALSE` until the API responds. | | ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx | Replaces explicit adapter-type allowlist with `capabilities.supportsInstructionsBundle` flag; correct for all built-in types and now extensible for external adapters. | | ui/src/components/AgentConfigForm.tsx | Replaces `NONLOCAL_TYPES` deny-list with capability-based `isLocal`; behavior is equivalent for built-in adapters; external adapters now require explicit flag declarations. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/adapters/process/index.ts Line: 5-9 Comment: **Missing explicit capability flags** `process` and `http` (`server/src/adapters/http/index.ts`) are the only built-in adapters that weren't updated with explicit flags. The behavior is correct (all flags `undefined` → `?? false` in `buildAdapterCapabilities`), but it leaves these two files inconsistent with every other built-in and makes their intended capabilities invisible to future readers without tracing through the fallback logic. ```suggestion export const processAdapter: ServerAdapterModule = { type: "process", execute, testEnvironment, models: [], supportsLocalAgentJwt: false, supportsInstructionsBundle: false, requiresMaterializedRuntimeSkills: false, agentConfigurationDoc: `# process agent configuration ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/adapters/use-adapter-capabilities.ts Line: 17-26 Comment: **`KNOWN_DEFAULTS` duplicates backend data and will drift** This static map must be manually kept in sync with `registry.ts` every time a built-in adapter is added or its capabilities change. Currently `process` and `http` are absent (correct — they get `ALL_FALSE`), but any new built-in adapter added to the registry will silently default to `ALL_FALSE` on the first render until someone remembers to update this map. A brief comment noting the maintenance requirement would help: ```suggestion /** * Synchronous fallback for known built-in adapter types so capability checks * return correct values on first render before the /api/adapters call resolves. * * Keep this in sync with server/src/adapters/registry.ts. Adapters not listed * here fall back to ALL_FALSE until the first /api/adapters response arrives. */ const KNOWN_DEFAULTS: Record = { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3543.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a 23-test auth validation matrix for `assertBoard`, `assertCompanyAccess`, and `getActorInfo`, plus agent governance files, PM2 config, and a lockfile cleanup commit. The test logic itself is accurate against the current `authz.ts` implementation, but there are two P1 issues that need resolving before merge: - The local `Actor` type in the test file introduces `\"board_key\"` as a valid `source` value, but `express.d.ts` does not include it. The test at lines 185–194 therefore exercises an impossible runtime input; either `express.d.ts` is incomplete or the test case should use `\"session\"`. - `ecosystem.config.cjs` and `.claude/commands/pm2-3100.md` / `pm2-all.md` hardcode `/Users/dev3/Desktop/paperclipai/` paths that will fail immediately for any other developer. Additionally, the PR test-plan checkbox \"No pnpm-lock.yaml changes ✓\" is factually incorrect — `pnpm-lock.yaml` is among the 14 changed files (via the `d23169ed` lockfile-cleanup commit). Per CONTRIBUTING.md, the PR description should also include a **thinking path** — covering why the approach was chosen, trade-offs considered, and any risks.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge as-is: one test case exercises an impossible source value due to Actor type drift, and machine-specific paths in ecosystem.config.cjs will break other developers. Two P1 findings: the "board_key" source value divergence between the local Actor type and express.d.ts makes a test case fictional rather than auditable, and the hardcoded /Users/dev3/ paths in ecosystem.config.cjs and two command files will fail for any other user. The test logic for the remaining 22 cases is correct and well-structured. server/src/__tests__/auth-validation-matrix.test.ts (board_key source divergence), ecosystem.config.cjs and .claude/commands/pm2-3100.md / pm2-all.md (hardcoded developer paths)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/__tests__/auth-validation-matrix.test.ts | 23-test auth validation matrix; the local Actor type silently adds "board_key" to the source union that is absent from express.d.ts, making one test case exercise a source value that cannot exist at runtime. | | ecosystem.config.cjs | PM2 ecosystem config with cwd and log paths hardcoded to /Users/dev3/, breaking startup for any other developer. | | .claude/commands/pm2-3100.md | Slash command cd path hardcoded to /Users/dev3/Desktop/paperclipai; same issue in pm2-all.md. | | .claude/scout-claude.md | Agent identity file for Scout QA lead; no issues. | | .claude/rex-claude.md | Agent identity file for Rex engineering lead; no issues. | | CLAUDE.md | Project-level Claude agent identity for Scout; no code issues. | | pnpm-lock.yaml | Lockfile updated (chore: clean lockfile for governance snapshot); PR test plan incorrectly claims no lockfile changes. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/auth-validation-matrix.test.ts Line: 21 Comment: **Local `Actor` type diverges from `express.d.ts` — `"board_key"` source value does not exist** The test's local `Actor` type adds `"board_key"` to the `source` union, but `server/src/types/express.d.ts` defines `source` as `"local_implicit" | "session" | "agent_key" | "agent_jwt" | "none"` — `"board_key"` is absent. The test at lines 185–194 constructs a request with `source: "board_key"`, which is an input that can never exist at runtime. 3544.body:
Greptile Summary

Adds a review-stage checkout rebind path so an assigned current review-participant can re-checkout an `in_review` issue from a new run and atomically rebind `executionRunId` without forcing the issue back to `in_progress`. Non-participants still receive the existing `409 Conflict`. The `test-embedded-postgres` temp-dir fix and the two new regression tests are clean additions.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; the only open finding is a narrow concurrent-retry race that is a hardening improvement, not a blocking bug. All remaining findings are P2. The core allow/block split logic is correct, the fallthrough to a 409 already protects against the race in the common case, and the regression tests cover both scenarios. server/src/services/issues.ts — the reviewCheckout UPDATE WHERE clause

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds review-stage checkout rebind path; core logic is sound but the UPDATE lacks an optimistic lock on the old executionRunId, creating a narrow concurrent-retry race. | | server/src/__tests__/issues-service.test.ts | Adds two regression tests covering the allow (assigned reviewer) and block (non-participant) checkout cases; setup/teardown mirrors existing test suites. | | packages/db/src/test-embedded-postgres.ts | Introduces a tempRoot wrapper around dataDir so the embedded-postgres instance has exclusive ownership of its directory; cleanup targets the right path in all code paths. | | docs/api/issues.md | Documents the new in_review rebind semantic; change is accurate and matches implemented behavior. | | doc/SPEC-implementation.md | Spec updated to describe the review-stage preservation invariant alongside existing checkout contract items. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issues.ts Line: 1827-1842 Comment: **Missing optimistic lock on `executionRunId` in review rebind UPDATE** The `reviewCheckout` UPDATE's WHERE clause only checks `status = "in_review"` and `assigneeAgentId = agentId`, without pinning the old `executionRunId`. If the same reviewer agent makes two concurrent checkout calls (e.g., retry after a timeout), both pass `isAssignedPendingExecutionParticipantCheckout`, and the second UPDATE blindly overwrites the first's bound `executionRunId`. Both callers receive a "success" response holding different run IDs, leaving an inconsistent ownership state. Adding an optimistic guard matches the pattern already used by the regular checkout's `executionLockCondition`. ```suggestion const reviewCheckout = await db .update(issues) .set({ executionRunId: checkoutRunId, executionLockedAt: now, updatedAt: now, }) .where( and( eq(issues.id, id), eq(issues.status, "in_review"), eq(issues.assigneeAgentId, agentId), current.executionRunId ? eq(issues.executionRunId, current.executionRunId) : isNull(issues.executionRunId), ), ) .returning() .then((rows) => rows[0] ?? null); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3547.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR improves the New Issue Dialog validation UX by adding an inline title warning, a shake animation on the submit button, an auto-dismissing amber banner, and a contextual tooltip — all good ideas. However, there is a logic bug that undermines the core UX goal: the `hasAttempted` state is set in `handleButtonClick` but is never included in the condition that renders the inline \"Issue title is required\" hint, so the red error text is visible immediately when the dialog first opens (before any user interaction). - **P1**: `hasAttempted && !title.trim()` should gate the inline hint in `NewIssueDialog.tsx` (line 1128) — the flag exists but is unused, making the error always visible on empty title. - The PR description is missing the **Risks** and **Model Used** sections required by the PR template, and no before/after screenshots are included for this visual change (both required by CONTRIBUTING.md).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until the `hasAttempted` guard is added to the inline error hint — without it the 'required' text shows on dialog open, breaking the intended UX improvement. One P1 logic bug: the `hasAttempted` flag is declared and set but never read in the JSX condition, so the inline error is always visible on an empty title rather than only after a submit attempt. Remaining findings are P2. Score is 4 rather than 5 because of the P1 issue. ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx — the inline warning condition on line 1128 needs `hasAttempted &&` prepended.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx | Adds inline validation UX (shake, warning banner, tooltip); `hasAttempted` flag is declared but never used to gate the inline error hint, so it shows immediately on dialog open — defeating the intended UX improvement. | | ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.test.tsx | Adds mocks for DialogTitle and Tooltip to keep existing tests green; no new tests cover the validation UX behavior introduced in this PR. | | ui/src/index.css | Adds `@keyframes shake` and `@keyframes fadeIn` at the bottom of the file; straightforward and correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx Line: 1128-1132 Comment: **`hasAttempted` flag is declared but never used to gate the inline warning** The "Issue title is required" hint renders immediately when the dialog opens because `hasAttempted` (which is set to `true` only inside `handleButtonClick`) is never included in the condition. On first render the title is empty, so the error text is always visible before the user has typed anything — the exact anti-pattern the PR is trying to fix. ```suggestion {hasAttempted && !title.trim() && (
Issue title is required
)} ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx Line: 1724-1741 Comment: **Shake/warning fires during pending state when title is empty** If `createIssue.isPending` is `true` and the title field is empty, clicking the button still triggers the shake animation and warning banner (since `handleButtonClick` only checks `!title.trim()`, not `isPending`). Adding a guard keeps the validation feedback from conflicting with the loading state. ```suggestion

3548.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds three additive optional fields required by the gemini-local adapter: `thinkingTokens` to `UsageSummary`, `retryDelaySec` to `AdapterExecutionResult`, and `"estimated_cost"` to both `AdapterBillingType` and `BILLING_TYPES` (with a matching UI display name). - The `normalizeLedgerBillingType` function in `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` maps raw adapter billing types to `BillingType` via an explicit `switch`; it has no case for `"estimated_cost"`, so any adapter reporting that value will have it silently stored as `"unknown"` in the ledger. This function needs a new `case \"estimated_cost\": return \"estimated_cost\"` branch.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge only after updating normalizeLedgerBillingType in heartbeat.ts to handle "estimated_cost". One P1 defect: estimated_cost is defined in the type system and constants but the heartbeat normalization function silently converts it to "unknown", making the new billing type functionally inert on the ledger side. The other two fields (thinkingTokens, retryDelaySec) are clean additive changes. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — normalizeLedgerBillingType needs a case for "estimated_cost"

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts | Adds thinkingTokens to UsageSummary, estimated_cost to AdapterBillingType, and retryDelaySec to AdapterExecutionResult — all additive, but estimated_cost is not handled in heartbeat.ts normalization | | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Adds "estimated_cost" to BILLING_TYPES constant and derived BillingType — correct additive change | | ui/src/lib/utils.ts | Adds display name mapping for estimated_cost in billingTypeDisplayName — exhaustive Record stays complete |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts Line: 42 Comment: **`estimated_cost` not handled in billing normalization** `normalizeLedgerBillingType` in `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` (lines 411–429) has an explicit `switch` for each known billing type and falls through to `return "unknown"` for anything else. Since `"estimated_cost"` is not listed as a case, any adapter that reports `billingType: "estimated_cost"` will have it silently recorded as `"unknown"` in the ledger. The new constant is effectively dead unless the normalization function is updated to pass it through. ```ts // heartbeat.ts – add this case to normalizeLedgerBillingType case "estimated_cost": return "estimated_cost"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
